body:
Fixes #526

## Summary
- Reworked code translation (`translate_program`) to route through a language-neutral **code meta language** (`CodeMeaning` / `formalize_code_meaning` / `render_code_meaning`) instead of direct `(source, target)` match arms. This removes the N² anti-pattern issue #526 explicitly forbids: the translator now stays at `N` formalizers + `N` renderers, so pairs that never had a hardcoded arm (Python → JavaScript, Rust → Go) translate through one shared meaning for free.
- Added issue #526 round-trip translation quality requirements, solution notes, online research, and raw PR/issue snapshots under `docs/case-studies/issue-526/`.
- Expanded natural-language translation coverage so every supported language surface formalizes to the meta language and every directed en/ru/hi/zh pair round-trips through the shared meaning (already routed through `TranslationPipeline`, not direct pairs).
- Added Rust → JavaScript → Rust code translation coverage plus a `code_translation_routes_through_the_code_meta_language_not_direct_pairs` test that proves never-hardcoded pairs translate through one shared `meaning:` link.
- Updated `VISION.md`, `ARCHITECTURE.md`, `ROADMAP.md`, `REQUIREMENTS.md`, and `CONTRIBUTING.md` so future translation work is governed by the same round-trip, meta-language rule.

## Root obsolete practice fixed
`translate_program` previously matched on the concrete `(source, target)` language pair — a direct pair-specific translator. Issue #526 states direct translation without a meta language is computationally unsolvable (N languages → N² translators). The fix formalizes any source fragment into a `CodeMeaning` (source language irrelevant), then renders that meaning into the requested target; no `(source, target)` arm survives.

## Reproduction
Before the fix, the reproducer failed because Rust → JavaScript returned a translation gap instead of an add-function renderer, and cross pairs like Python → JavaScript were never supported:

```bash
cargo test --test unit rust_javascript_code_translation_round_trips_through_code_meaning -- --nocapture
cargo test --test unit code_translation_routes_through_the_code_meta_language_not_direct_pairs -- --nocapture
```

## Verification
Post-merge verification on branch `issue-526-215ae32f521b`:

```bash
cargo test --test unit translation_round_trip -- --nocapture
cargo test --test unit translation_via_links -- --nocapture
cargo test --test unit issue_526_translation_quality_documents_are_traceable -- --nocapture
cargo fmt --check
rust-script scripts/check-file-size.rs
cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features
cargo test
```

## Visuals
Not applicable; this change is tests, translation helper behavior, and documentation.

body:
## Summary

Fixes #556. Generalizes the response-language follow-up from a single
repository-lookup special case into the **whole class** of "re-answer the
previous request in another language" turns, driven entirely by the
data-grounded seed lexicon and expressed as a grounded meta-algorithm.

A bare follow-up such as *"I do not understand English, write in Russian"* is not
a new question — it asks the assistant to re-answer the previous request in a
named language. Rather than localizing one handler, the follow-up now **replays
the previous user request through the entire solver** with the target language
forced at a single detection seam, so every localizable answer family
(project lookup, capabilities, identity, concept lookup, …) re-renders in the
requested language — and reverses back to English on request.

## What changed

- **Single forced-language seam** (`src/language.rs`): a thread-local override
  consulted at the top of `language::detect`, installed by an RAII guard
  (`set_forced_language`). One seam localizes the whole solver — no per-handler
  threading. Mirrored in JS as a module-global `FORCED_RESPONSE_LANGUAGE`
  consulted at the top of `detectLanguage`.
- **Whole-solver replay** (`src/solver_handlers/response_language_followup.rs`):
  recovers the prior user request from history and replays it with the target
  language forced. Recursion-guarded (a replay already carries a forced language)
  and inconclusive-guarded (unknown/ill_formed/clarify replays fall through).
- **Seed-grounded triggers** (issue #386 convention): the requested language and
  the comprehension-failure marker are recognised by *role* from
  `data/seed/meanings-translation.lino`, grounded in Wikidata
  (`understanding` Q46744; English Q1860, Russian Q7737, Hindi Q1568,
  Chinese Q7850) — never a hardcoded phrase table.
- **Grounded meta-algorithm recipe**
  (`data/meta/response-language-followup-recipe.lino`): a machine-readable,
  eight-step description of the universal recursive-reasoning algorithm, pinned
  by `tests/unit/specification/response_language_meta_algorithm.rs` (8 grounding
  tests) so the recipe can never silently drift from the live source. Documented
  in `docs/meta-algorithm.md`.

## Testing (every finest detail, all four languages)

- `tests/unit/specification/response_language_followup.rs` — capabilities and
  identity re-render in ru/hi/zh, reverse to English on request, and retarget
  between two non-English languages (Russian context → Chinese) proving the seam
  is target-driven and language-agnostic.
- `tests/unit/specification/project_lookups.rs` — the original issue #556
  repository lookup across all four seeded languages.
- `tests/unit/specification/translation_round_trip.rs` — issue #526 round-trip:
  English↔Russian/Hindi/Chinese vocabulary survives a
  source→meta-language→target→source cycle with both meaning and surface
  preserved.
- `tests/unit/specification/response_language_meta_algorithm.rs` — 8 tests
  grounding the recipe against the live source (steps, roles, Wikidata cache,
  functions, seam, Rust↔JS parity, pinning tests).
- `experiments/js_worker_followup_harness.mjs` — 5/5 JS worker cases
  (project_lookup / capabilities / identity across ru/hi/zh + cross-language),
  proving Rust↔JS parity.

Full suite: **1245 unit tests pass**; `cargo fmt --check` and `cargo clippy
--all-targets` clean.

## Issue #556 requirements → evidence

| Requirement | Where |
| --- | --- |
| Generalize to the whole class in all languages | whole-solver replay + `response_language_followup.rs` (4 langs) |
| Universal meta-algorithm, recursive steps in meta language | `response-language-followup-recipe.lino` + `response_language_meta_algorithm.rs` |
| Meanings grounded in external data sources | seed roles grounded in Wikidata Q46744/Q1860/Q7737/Q1568/Q7850 |
| Full meta-language translation cycle | `translate_via_default_pipeline` round-trips through the meta language |
| Issue #526 round-trip testing | `translation_round_trip.rs` |
| Every finest detail tested / JS parity | 1245 unit tests + 5/5 JS harness cases |
title: Generalize response-language follow-ups to the whole request class (issue #556)

## Summary

Resolves the umbrella translation issue #218 and the follow-up PR feedback by fixing the reported apple translation bugs and adding supported-language coverage guards so future feature fixes cannot silently cover only English/Russian.

- Fixes #216 — `translate apple to russian` now returns `яблоко` instead of `[ru]`.
- Fixes #217 — `переведи «яблоко» на английский` now returns `apple` instead of `"[en] яблоко"`.
- Extends unquoted translation prompts across all supported languages: English, Russian, Hindi, and Chinese.
- Adds CI coverage checks for tool localization, feature-capability test matrices, concept-context labels, and browser translation registry entries across every supported language.

## What changed

### Rust core
- `src/translation/prompt.rs`: extracts unquoted translation surfaces from English, Russian, Hindi, and Chinese prompt forms.
- `src/solver_helpers.rs`: detects native source/target language markers and infers source language from the quoted/unquoted surface before falling back to instruction language.
- `src/solver_handlers/mod.rs`: keeps Hindi/Chinese `translate` capability questions out of the translation handler unless a target language is present.
- `examples/refresh_translation_cache.rs` and `data/translation-cache/`: seed single-noun `apple` targets for Russian, Hindi, and Chinese.

### Browser worker
- Mirrors the Rust prompt extraction and source/target detection in `src/web/formal_ai_worker.js`.
- Extends `TRANSLATION_MEANING_REGISTRY` so browser fallback meanings cover English, Russian, Hindi, and Chinese.

### Tests and CI guards
- `tests/unit/specification/translation_via_links.rs`: covers unquoted `apple` translation into every supported target plus native Hindi/Chinese prompt forms.
- `tests/e2e/tests/issue-218.spec.js`: browser regressions cover English/Russian/Hindi/Chinese targets and native Hindi/Chinese prompts.
- `tests/unit/specification/capabilities.rs`: every feature capability is tested in every supported language.
- `tests/e2e/scripts/check-multilingual-intent-coverage.mjs`: CI now verifies supported-language coverage for localized responses, concepts, concept contexts, tools, feature capabilities, prompt-pattern groups, and browser translation meanings.

### Case study
- `docs/case-studies/issue-218/` documents the timeline, root causes, online references, before/after behavior, and verification evidence.

## Before / after

| Prompt | Before | After |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `translate apple to russian` | `[ru]` | `яблоко` |
| `Translate Apple to Russian` | `[ru]` | `Яблоко` |
| `переведи «яблоко» на английский` | `[en] яблоко` | `apple` |
| `translate apple to hindi` | `[hi]` / fallback | `सेब` |
| `translate apple to chinese` | `[zh]` / fallback | `蘋果` / `苹果` |
| `apple का हिंदी में अनुवाद करो` | unknown fallback | `सेब` |
| `把 apple 翻译成中文` | unknown fallback | `蘋果` / `苹果` |

## Test plan

- [x] `rust-script scripts/check-file-size.rs`
- [x] `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- [x] `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features`
- [x] `cargo test`

- [x] `npm run --prefix tests/e2e check:intent-coverage`

## Upstream reports

None filed. The defects were local to this repository: Wiktionary serves the needed translation tables, Wikidata lexemes are linked, and the missing pieces were local cache coverage, prompt extraction, browser fallback data, and multilingual CI guards.

body:
## Summary
- Replace the robotic `meaning: …\nsurface (…): …` translation body with the deformalized target surface (still quoted when the user quoted the source), so the assistant reads like natural conversation.
- Resolve any surface pair through a real `formalize → meaning → deformalize` pipeline backed by Wiktionary translation tables and Wikidata sense joins — not a hand-written phrase list. The pipeline lives under `src/translation/` (`http`, `cache`, `wiktionary`, `wikidata`, `meaning`, `pipeline`, `formatting`).
- Cache raw HTTP responses under `data/translation-cache/<fnv1a>.body` + `.url` via `CachedHttpClient` so unit tests run deterministically offline against the committed cache; live mode is gated by `FORMAL_AI_LIVE_API` and can be refreshed with `examples/refresh_translation_cache.rs`.
- Add `match_source_formatting` / `matchSourceFormatting` helpers so the source fragment's leading-letter case and terminal punctuation survive the round-trip (`как у тебя дела?` → `how are you?`, `Как у тебя дела` → `How are you`).
- Document the change with R213/R214/R215 in `REQUIREMENTS.md`, the pipeline + browser fallback in `ARCHITECTURE.md` §10, the surface-preservation invariant in `VISION.md`, and a full case study under `docs/case-studies/issue-207/`.

Fixes #207.

## Reproduction
Before: the prompt `Переведи "как у тебя дела?" на английский.` produced a robotic
```
meaning: meaning_2cfc55c914d57d9e
surface (ru): как у тебя дела?
surface (en): How are you?
```
After: the assistant answers
```
"how are you?"
```
with the meaning id + source/target slugs preserved in the Links Notation trace (`evidence_links`) rather than in the human-readable body. Lowercase source → lowercase target; capitalized source → capitalized target. The pipeline no longer relies on a hand-written list: any surface routed through `TranslationPipeline::translate` resolves via Wiktionary + Wikidata.

## Architecture
The pipeline (`src/translation/pipeline.rs`):
1. Fetches the source-edition Wiktionary page and parses its `{{trans-top}}` blocks for `target_lang` candidates.
2. Falls back to the `/translations` subpage when the main page omits translations.
3. Falls back to the target-edition Wiktionary page in reverse when the source edition is sparse (typical for ru → en).
4. Generates phrasal variants (e.g. dropping Russian "у тебя", "у вас", "у меня" infixes) when the literal page does not exist.
5. Selects the best sense block by round-trip confirmation rate.
6. Upgrades the meaning id to a Wikidata Q-item / sense id via the canonical `ontolex:sense / wdt:P5137` lexeme join.

The Rust pipeline is the canonical implementation. The browser worker (`src/web/formal_ai_worker.js`) cannot reach Wiktionary or Wikidata directly because of browser CORS restrictions, so it keeps a small offline phrase table as a CORS-safe fallback for the GitHub Pages demo.

## Test plan
- [x] `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- [x] `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
- [x] `cargo test` (406 unit tests passing, 69 ignored as tracked future work)
- [x] New unit tests in `tests/unit/specification/translation_via_links.rs` cover (a) lowercase preservation, (b) terminal-punctuation preservation when absent in source, (c) eight unrelated phrase pairs across en/ru/hi/zh routed entirely through cached real-world responses.
- [x] `tests/unit/docs_requirements.rs::issue_207_natural_translation_documents_are_present_and_traceable` ensures REQUIREMENTS.md / ARCHITECTURE.md / VISION.md / case-study stay in sync with the new architecture.

## Notes for reviewers
- The first attempt at a "shared offline meaning registry" was rejected by the maintainer as a fake solution. The new implementation removes that registry entirely and routes every translation through Wiktionary's translation tables and Wikidata's SPARQL lexeme join.
- Hash invariants for already-issued ids are preserved: surfaces still hash through the same `stable_id` scheme; the Wikidata Q-id upgrade happens at the `MeaningId` layer when the lexeme join succeeds.
- Cached responses are committed (206 files under `data/translation-cache/`) so CI is offline and deterministic; contributors can refresh them with `FORMAL_AI_LIVE_API=1 cargo run --example refresh_translation_cache`.
title: feat: generalized translation via Wiktionary + Wikidata pipeline

body:
## Summary

Fixes #221 — stops faking translation for common nouns. The Rust
pipeline and the browser demo now translate any reachable noun through
the real `source → formalize → meaning → deformalize → target` path
using only Wikipedia / Wikidata / Wiktionary data. No LLM in the loop,
no pre-extracted dictionary in the repo.

**This revision (2026-05-22) follows the PR review feedback:** the
previous 128-entry offline dictionary is gone. The only legal cached
artefact is now a **verbatim API response body** stored as
**human-readable raw text** inside indented Links Notation files under
`data/seed/api-cache/`, capped at 128 records per semantic bucket and
1500 lines per file. Bodies use Links Notation's doubled-quote escape
(one literal `"` is written as two consecutive `"`); no other character
is escaped, so reviewers can read the raw API JSON directly. Anything
that cannot be served from the bundle falls through to the on-disk
accelerator (gitignored) and then to live HTTP. The browser worker has
its own live fallback so it never needs a bundled dictionary.

Full investigation lives in `docs/case-studies/issue-221/`
(timeline through 2026-05-22, eleven verbatim requirements, five root
causes, fixes, before/after table, verification commands, upstream
defect notes, and online research on Wiktionary CORS / Wikidata SPARQL
polysemy).

## Changes

### Raw API-response seed bundle (replaces the dictionary)
- `data/seed/api-cache/*.lino` — verbatim Wikidata and Wiktionary API
  response bodies, indented Links Notation with **plain-text** payloads
  (chunks split at ≤200 chars; values use Links Notation's
  doubled-quote escape — one literal `"` becomes `""`). Bundle is
  capped at 128 records per bucket (entities, properties, search,
  sparql, pages-per-language) and every file stays under 1500 lines;
  oversize bodies split into `<bucket>-partN.lino` parts that re-join
  at load by URL.
- `build.rs` enumerates the `.lino` files at compile time and emits
  `seed_bundle_files.rs` into `OUT_DIR`; `src/translation/cache.rs`
  picks it up via `include!(concat!(env!("OUT_DIR"), ...))` so adding
  a new part file is automatic — no per-file `include_str!` edit.
- `examples/refresh_translation_cache.rs` drives the full pipeline
  against a curated 128-noun seed list, populates the on-disk
  accelerator, then rebundles into the committed `.lino` files
  (splitting oversize records, cleaning stale parts).
- `experiments/convert_base64_seed_to_text.rs` — one-shot migration
  tool used to rewrite the historical base64-chunked bundle into the
  new plain-text layout. Kept for reproducibility.
- **Deleted**: `data/seed/translations.lino`,
  `src/translation/dictionary.rs`, `examples/build_translation_dictionary.rs`.

### Semantic-identity HTTP cache
- `src/translation/cache.rs` keys URLs by **kind of data**, not URL
  hash: `data/wikidata-cache/{search,entities,query,sparql}/`,
  `data/wiktionary-cache/<lang>/`, `data/http-cache/misc/`. All three
  roots are gitignored — the on-disk cache is a local accelerator
  written by `FORMAL_AI_LIVE_API=1` runs; the committed source of
  truth is the seed bundle above.
- `CachedHttpClient::get` consults three layers in order: seed bundle
  → on-disk accelerator → live HTTP.

### Rust pipeline
- `src/translation/wikidata.rs` — SPARQL lexeme join now requires
  `wikibase:lexicalCategory` to match between source and target
  lexemes, so polysemous surfaces like `water` no longer cross
  noun ↔ verb boundaries (was `water → поливать`, now `water → вода`).
- `src/translation/pipeline.rs` — Stage 1a follows
  `{{see translation subpage|...}}` to the `/translations` subpage
  when the main page only carries the marker.
  `FORMAL_AI_TRANSLATION_DEBUG=1` enables verbose stage-by-stage
  tracing (closes the issue #218 future-work item).

### Browser worker
- `src/web/formal_ai_worker.js` — removed `TRANSLATION_DICTIONARY` and
  `lookupDictionary`. Added `liveWiktionaryTranslate(surface, source,
  target)` that calls
  `*.wiktionary.org/w/api.php?action=parse&page=...&prop=wikitext&format=json&origin=*`
  directly, follows `{{see translation subpage|...}}` to the
  `/translations` subpage, and extracts the `{{tt+|<lang>|...}}` /
  `{{t|...}}` template payload. `translateSurface` and `tryTranslation`
  are now async; the only caller already runs inside `async`.
- `src/web/seed_loader.js` — removed the `extractTranslations` parser
  and the `translations` field; the worker no longer hydrates an
  offline dictionary.
- Mobile-friendly by design: nothing pre-bundled, the MediaWiki action
  API is CORS-friendly through `origin=*`, the seed bundle keeps the
  worker payload tiny.

### Tests & docs
- `tests/unit/specification/translation_via_links.rs` — `issue_221_*`
  regressions covering quoted + unquoted common-noun translation in
  both directions.
- `tests/unit/data_files.rs` — verifies every shipped `.lino` file
  parses through `lino_objects_codec::format::parse_indented`, stays
  under 1500 lines, and never falls back to typed-object encoding.
- `tests/unit/docs_requirements.rs` — adds `data/seed/api-cache` to
  the `is_skipped_tree` allowlist so the deferred-labels scan does
  not trip on verbatim Wikipedia wikitext that legitimately contains
  forbidden substrings (`mvp`, `poc`, etc.).
- `tests/e2e/tests/issue-221.spec.js` — Playwright coverage of the
  live-worker path: quoted RU→EN, quoted EN→RU, unquoted prompts,
  Russian inflected forms via MediaWiki redirect, round-trip stability.
- `docs/case-studies/issue-221/{README.md,online-research.md}` —
  rewritten for the API-cache + live-worker architecture; preserves
  the original investigation timeline and adds the 2026-05-22 entries.
- `changelog.d/20260522_000500_issue_221_translation_common_nouns.md`
  — rewritten changelog fragment.

## Before / After

| Prompt | Before (v0.100.0) | After |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `Переведи "помидор" на английский.` | `"[en] помидор"` | `"tomato"` |
| `Переведи "огурец" на английский.` | `"[en] огурец"` | `"cucumber"` |
| `переведи "картофель" на английский` | `"[en] картофель"` | `"potato"` |
| `translate "tomato" to russian` | `"[ru] tomato"` | `"помидор"` |
| `translate "carrot" to russian` | `"[ru] carrot"` | `"морковь"` |
| `translate "water" to russian` | `"поливать"` (verb!) | `"вода"` |
| `переведи помидор на английский` (unquoted) | `"[en] помидор"` | `"tomato"` |

## Test plan

- [x] `cargo build --release` — clean.
- [x] `cargo clippy --release --all-targets -- -D warnings` — clean.
- [x] `cargo fmt --check` — clean.
- [x] `cargo test --release` — 229 lib + 11 integration + 462 unit
  tests pass, including the `issue_221_*` regressions, the existing
  issue #218 coverage, and the new `lino_data_files_are_parseable_…`
  parser-roundtrip check on the regenerated seed bundle.
- [x] `rust-script scripts/check-file-size.rs` — all shipped files
  inside their configured caps.
- [x] `FORMAL_AI_LIVE_API=1 cargo run --release --example refresh_translation_cache`
  — every seeded entry resolved; bundle re-written under
  `data/seed/api-cache/` within the 128-record / 1500-line caps.
- [ ] CI green on `windows-latest`, `macos-latest`, `ubuntu-latest`,
  plus Lint, Format, Coverage, and E2E (waiting on this push).
- [ ] `npm --prefix tests/e2e run test:local -- tests/issue-221.spec.js`
  — exercises the live Wiktionary fallback in the browser worker
  (requires network access during the run).

Fixes #221


## CI follow-ups (2026-05-22, after re-run)

Four small fixes layered on top of the seed-bundle rewrite so the
pipeline survives the live-API-first browser path on CI's network and
honours the reviewer's "must be human-readable" requirement:

- **`build.rs` — preserve chunk order across split records.** The
  lexicographic `files.sort()` placed `wiktionary-pages-part10.lino`
  before `-part2.lino` and the bare `wiktionary-pages.lino` (chunk 0)
  after every part, so any record split into multiple `.lino` files
  had its chunks reassembled out of order. Sort by
  `(bucket, part_index)` instead. Only split records were affected
  (apple failed; tomato fit in a single chunk).
- **`extractWiktionaryTranslation` — handle Russian Wiktionary
  blocks.** Russian Wiktionary uses
  `{{перев-блок|<sense>\n|<lang>=[[surface]]\n|...}}` rather than the
  English `{{t|<lang>|...}}` template family. The worker now matches
  both. Also added an NFD-based combining-mark strip so English
  Wiktionary's `{{t+|ru|помидо́р}}` (with U+0301 acute) resolves to the
  expected `помидор`.
- **`liveWiktionaryTranslate` — parallelize direct fetch and lemma
  resolution.** Inflected forms like `помидоры` need a 404, an
  OpenSearch round-trip to the lemma `помидор`, and a fetch of that
  lemma page. Running the first fetch and the OpenSearch lookup via
  `Promise.all` saves one sequential round-trip and keeps the assertion
  under Playwright's 5 s `expect` cap in CI.
- **`data/seed/api-cache/*.lino` — store responses as plain text.**
  Reviewer feedback: "We should not encode raw API data in .lino files
  as base64, it should be all human readable." Cache reader/writer
  now use Links Notation's doubled-quote escape (`""` represents one
  literal `"`) instead of base64; chunker never starts a chunk with
  `"` so split points always land on the safe side of an LN delimiter
  run. Wiktionary bucket shrinks from 18 to 4 parts (raw text is ~⅓
  the size of its base64 form).
title: Replace pre-seeded dictionary with raw API-response cache (issue #221)

body:
## Summary

Fixes #676.

Many commands and prompts failed when Formal AI was reached through an agentic CLI (OpenCode). Every failure traced back to the same class of root cause on the **Formal AI side**: a slightly different phrasing than a seed anticipated fell through to the *unknown* fallback, and the reasoning trace shown to the client was an identical robotic category template. OpenCode itself behaves correctly — it forwards the prompt and renders whatever the server returns (see [`upstream.md`](docs/case-studies/issue-676/upstream.md)).

Each fix is applied to **both** the Rust engine and the JS web worker mirror, and is covered by unit + e2e tests.

## Requirements addressed

The issue's asks were extracted into stable IDs in the [case study](docs/case-studies/issue-676/README.md):

| ID | Requirement | Status |
|----|-------------|--------|
| **R1** | `execute pwd` (and other shell tokens) in agentic mode | ✅ Fixed |
| **R2** | Natural-language file listing (`give me a list of files…`) | ✅ Fixed |
| **R3** | Naming (`Now your name is Ineffa`) + later recall | ✅ Fixed |
| **R4** | `How are you?` should not return the generic greeting | ✅ Fixed |
| **R5** | Self-healing on natural self-directed repair requests | ✅ Fixed |
| **R6** | Support as many message variations as possible | ✅ Broadened + tested |
| **R7** | Auto-learning / meta self-solve | ✅ Verified (self-heal + learning loop) |
| **R8** | Higher-quality, human-like thinking display | ✅ Fixed (primary focus) |
| **R9** | Deep case study in `./docs/case-studies/issue-676` | ✅ Delivered |
| **R10** | Debug/verbose tracing where root cause wasn't obvious | ✅ Added |
| **R11** | Report upstream issues if caused by another project | ✅ Triaged — no upstream bug |
| **R12** | Apply fixes across the whole codebase (Rust + JS) | ✅ Parity enforced by CI |
| **R13** | Land everything in this single PR | ✅ |

## What changed

### Agentic terminal commands (R1, R2)
`src/agentic_coding/planner.rs` now runs **any** seed shell token (`pwd`, `git`, `cargo`, `whoami`, …) named in a prompt, not just `ls`. `execute pwd`, `run git status`, and their many phrasings map to the real command. Natural-language file-listing requests resolve to `ls` across far more phrasings.

### Naming + recall (R3)
`src/solver_handlers/conversation_memory/` honours being named in conversation ("Now your name is Ineffa", "I'll call you Ada", "you are called …") and recalls it when later asked "what is your name", using dialog-local memory with **no server state** — mirrored in the browser worker.

### `How are you?` small talk (R4)
A dedicated `wellbeing` intent is matched **before** `greeting` (first-match-wins), so "how are you", "как дела", "आप कैसे हैं", and "你好吗" get an actual warm answer across en/ru/hi/zh instead of the generic greeting.

### Self-healing (R5, R7)
`src/agentic_coding/self_heal.rs` now triggers on natural self-directed repair requests ("Can you fix it yourself?", "debug yourself", "heal yourself") while ordinary "fix this file" requests stay out of the repair loop. Verified against the existing self-healing + learning-ledger loop.

### Human thinking display (R8 — primary focus)
The reasoning trace used to be a fixed six-line template ("Read the request … Detect the request language …") that differed between unrelated prompts only by a route label buried mid-list — which is exactly what OpenCode surfaced as a robotic "Thought" block.

Now every trace **opens with a per-intent, first-person narrative** of what the assistant understood and decided ("You asked how I'm doing, so I told you and offered to help."), with the concrete steps preserved beneath it as an expandable, **recursive "robotic detail"** layer. Applied to:
- the API/CLI `reasoning` field that agentic clients render (`src/thinking.rs` `thinking_narrative` → `render_thinking_steps`);
- the web thinking preview (`src/web/app/main.jsx` `ThinkingPreview`) across **en/ru/zh/hi**, without breaking issue-488's exact step counts (a separately-testid'd headline element).

### Message-variation coverage (R6)
Each fix ships a broad set of phrasings plus unit tests asserting many variants (`data/seed/greetings.lino`, `intent-routing.lino`, `prompt-patterns.lino`, `multilingual-responses.lino`).

### Code organization (keep the 1000-line CI gate green)
The R1/R2 growth pushed two files over the repository's 1000-line hard limit, so the self-contained clusters were extracted into sibling submodules — pure code movement, public paths unchanged:
- `src/agentic_coding/shell_command.rs` — the data-driven shell resolution split out of `planner.rs` (1142 → 899 lines).
- `src/solver_helpers/code.rs` — the code/program-translation cluster split out of `solver_helpers` (1042 → 719 lines); `solver_helpers.rs` becomes a directory module re-exporting the submodule so `crate::solver_helpers::*` paths stay stable.

## Tests

- **Rust**: full `cargo test` suite green — **2094 passed, 0 failed, 2 ignored** across all binaries (count reflects the merge of the latest `main`). New: `tests/unit/issue_676_naming.rs`, `tests/unit/issue_676_thinking_narrative.rs`, plus additions to `agentic_coding.rs`, `formal_ai.rs`, `multilingual_variations.rs`. Regenerated the self-AST / self-healing census golden fixtures after the planner source change (formatting + module extraction) so they match a fresh parse.
- **e2e**: `tests/e2e/tests/issue-676-thinking-narrative.spec.js` (3 tests) — narrative headline leads the trace and robotic detail survives on expand; greeting vs wellbeing get distinct headlines; calculation route reads as a calculation. Registered in `playwright.local.config.js`.
- **Gates**: `cargo fmt --check` clean, `cargo clippy --all-targets` 0 warnings, total-reference-closure `unresolved: {}`, i18n catalog check passes (4 locales, 409 keys), multilingual intent-coverage parity green.

## Case study & upstream triage (R9, R11)

Full root-cause analysis, evidence images, and verification strategy live in [`docs/case-studies/issue-676/`](docs/case-studies/issue-676/). Upstream triage in [`upstream.md`](docs/case-studies/issue-676/upstream.md) concludes the defects are Formal-AI-side; OpenCode forwards and renders correctly, so no upstream report is warranted.
title: Fix commands/prompts failing via OpenCode: agentic shell, naming, wellbeing, self-heal, human thinking trace (issue #676)

body:
## Summary

Fully resolves issue #386 in this single PR. The headline bug — the follow-up
**"Отмени сортировку"** ("cancel the sorting") returning `intent: unknown` — is
**fixed**, not deferred. It is fixed the way the issue asks: by an **architecture
rethink (R-h)**, not a hardcoded special case. Alongside it ship the three
report/UI asks from the issue description, a corrected deep case study, and — per
the maintainer's escalated scope — a **whole-codebase rethink that makes the seed
data self-describing**, so handlers reason over language-independent concepts
referenced by semantic role rather than raw words hardcoded in one language.

An earlier note on this PR root-caused the cancel-sort bug and scoped it as a
*follow-up epic*. Per the maintainer's instruction to **defer nothing and
implement everything here**, that deferral is reversed: the fix, its
cross-runtime mirror, its regression tests, and the corrected case study are all
in this PR.

Fixes #386

## The cancel-sort fix — inverse derivation, nothing hardcoded (R-h)

The program-modification ontology was **additive-only**: it could *add* a
modifier (`+path_argument`, `+reverse_sort`) but had no concept of a
*subtractive* one, so "cancel X" matched nothing and fell through to `unknown`.
The single root cause surfaced in three layers — the bare-follow-up coreference
gate, the modifier vocabulary, and the rule-synthesis decomposition.

Rather than hardcode "cancel" handling, **operations now declare their inverse in
seed data**, and the subtractive substitution rules are **derived at runtime** by
mirroring the additive ones:

- **Seed declares the inverse** — `data/seed/operation-vocabulary.lino` gains
  `operation "cancel_reverse_sort"` carrying `inverse "reverse_sort"` (line 167),
  with native phrasings in English, Russian, Hindi, and Chinese.
- **Rules are derived, not written** — `derive_inverse_rules()` in
  `src/program_plan.rs` takes each additive rule (`request:modifier → base_op`)
  and emits its mirror with add/remove swapped
  (`cancel_reverse_sort__reverse_sort_list_files`,
  `cancel_reverse_sort__reverse_sort_list_files_arg`). `program-plan-rules.lino`
  is **unchanged** — no new hand-written rules.
- **The coreference gate generalizes** — `src/program_coreference.rs` recognizes
  cancel/undo verbs and sort nouns (all four languages) as program follow-ups,
  so the cancel turn reaches the accumulated program instead of being rejected.
- **Rule synthesis verifies the inverse** — `src/rule_synthesis.rs` decomposes
  `cancel_reverse_sort` as the inverse of `reverse_sort` and flips its
  render/verification check to require ascending order again.

The effect: a "cancel X" follow-up lowers the accumulated program back *through*
"X", restoring the ascending sort **while keeping earlier edits** such as the
path argument. **Adding a new cancellable operation is now pure seed data with no
new control flow** — exactly the "nothing hardcoded in the code" requirement.

The web worker (`src/web/formal_ai_worker.js`) mirrors the same derivation so the
browser demo behaves identically to the native solver.

## The deep rethink — self-describing seed, handlers reason over concepts (R-h, whole codebase)

Per maintainer feedback ("I expect the entire codebase to be touched … reference
not raw words in single language, but concepts/meanings, translatable to any
language … all handlers should actually do reasoning, nothing should be
hardcoded"), the conversion is **not scoped to the cancel-sort bug** — it sweeps
the whole repository.

- **Concepts, not words.** Every recognizer that used to match hardcoded literals
  in one language now queries the seed lexicon by **semantic role** or **stable
  slug / language-independent id** (e.g. `lexicon().meanings_with_role(ROLE_…)`).
  Production code never compares against a raw word; the words live in
  `data/seed/*.lino` as `meaning`s with multilingual `lexeme`/`word` forms.
- **Self-describing & recursive.** Each `meaning` carries a `gloss`, every `word`
  form carries its own `description`, and each meaning is rooted — `defined_by`
  chains up to the **`link` root of the ontology** — so the data describes itself
  instead of relying on a parse order baked into the code. Library gates enforce
  this on every build: `every_meaning_is_self_describing`,
  `every_word_form_is_described`, `every_meaning_reaches_the_link_root`, and
  `every_meaning_covers_all_supported_languages` (en/ru/hi/zh, unconditional).
- **Rooted in external systems' APIs.** Meanings reference and pre-cache external
  knowledge bases: a Wiktionary page cache
  (`data/seed/api-cache/wiktionary-pages*.lino`) and, as the **flagship** of this
  PR, **Wikidata**. `src/translation/formalization.rs` — which still held the last
  hardcoded English label/property tables — now projects them from
  `data/seed/meanings-wikidata.lino`, where each entity/property records its
  **language-independent Q-id / P-id** (apple→Q89, subclass-of→P279, …), its
  English label, and its multilingual word forms. The tables are built once
  (`OnceLock`) and queried by role (`ROLE_WIKIDATA_ENTITY_ANCHOR`,
  `ROLE_BINARY_RELATION_PROPERTY`, `ROLE_TRANSLATION_PROPERTY`); the P31→P279
  copular reading is itself data-driven via each surface's action slug.
- **Scope.** Recognizer clusters across intent routing, units, calendar, fact
  relations, program/project, web-search, translation, calculation/arithmetic,
  summarization, the coding catalog, self-awareness, behavior rules and skill
  compilation are now seed-driven (see case study §4 and the changelog). Tests
  keep hardcoded examples — production code does not.
- **Files kept under the guard.** Growth from the conversion pushed three files
  past the 1000-line size guard; they are split with **no behaviour change**:
  `src/seed/meanings.rs` → `meanings/tests.rs`; `src/seed/roles.rs` →
  `roles/{program,intent,language,reasoning,tooling}.rs` (thin parent re-exports);
  and the code-generation unit tests into a `code_generation/` directory module.

## Report trimming & UI features (issue Description)

### Report trimming
- **Omit default-valued settings** — Mode, Status, Diagnostics, Theme, Guess
  probability, Temperature, Follow-up probability, and inference-only Location
  are no longer printed when equal to their shipped default (read from the single
  `PREFERENCE_DEFAULTS` source via `settingIsDefault`).
- **Fold the worker into the version** — `0.176.0 (wasm)` instead of a separate
  worker line.
- **Shorten "Attach full memory (optional)"** — a one-line pointer to
  `docs/upload-memory.md` instead of the zip/Gist walkthrough.
- **Drop the Reasoning Trace when the dialog is incomplete** — omitted if earlier
  turns were trimmed to fit GitHub's URL cap.

### New UI features
- **Reset settings** — reset each setting to its default individually, or all at
  once, from the settings panel; reset-all disables and an empty-state shows when
  nothing is modified.
- **Copy a conversation as Markdown** — copy the full dialog from the
  conversations list; with diagnostics mode on, reasoning steps fold in after
  each AI message.

## Case study & architecture (`docs/case-studies/issue-386/`)

Raw data, reconstructed timeline, the full lettered requirements list, a
**corrected** root-cause analysis (the additive-only ontology; the program state
*is* accumulated across turns via `active_program_context()`, added for #357),
and the **implemented** inverse-derivation fix written up step by step. The
broader architecture vision (R-h, self-describing seed data) is mapped to the
existing vision track for the parts beyond this operation.

## Reproduction

A four-turn Russian dialog builds a reverse-sorted, path-argument file lister,
then sends **"Отмени сортировку"**. Before: `intent: unknown`. After: the program
re-emits with the sort removed and the path argument intact. Full timeline in the
case study §1.

## Tests

Nothing is hardcoded in production code (operation vocabulary, inverse, and rules
are seed-driven; report defaults come from `PREFERENCE_DEFAULTS`). Hardcoded
examples live **only** in tests, which lock every feature against regression.

- **Rust integration** (`tests/integration/issue_386_cancel_sort.rs`) — 3 tests:
  the four-turn Russian repro must not be `unknown`; the full diagnostic
  reasoning chain (selected rule → coreference rewrite → derived candidate
  `cancel_reverse_sort__reverse_sort_list_files_arg` → `resolved_task
  list_files_arg`); and the cancel is undone in **English, Russian, Hindi, and
  Chinese**.
- **Rust unit** — `operation_vocabulary.rs`, `program_plan.rs` (asserts exactly
  the derived rule set, the round-trip inverse, and pure-data derivation),
  `program_coreference.rs`, `rule_synthesis.rs`.
- **e2e** (`tests/e2e/tests/issue-386.spec.js`) — 9 tests across 4 describe
  blocks (trimmed report ×3, reset settings ×2, copy conversation ×2, **cancel a
  program modification ×2**, including the inverse-rule reasoning chain).
- **Repro/diagnostic example** — `examples/issue_386_diagnostic_trace.rs`.

### Local verification (mirrors every CI gate)
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` ✓ · `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features`
  (passes even with `-D warnings`) ✓
- `cargo test --all-features` ✓ (378 lib + 20 integration + 760 unit, 1 ignored)
  · `cargo test --doc` ✓ · `rust-script scripts/check-file-size.rs` ✓
- `rust-script` guards replicated: file-size, changelog-fragment, version-modification ✓
- `bun run build:web` → bundles current (no drift) ✓
- i18n catalog, language-change parity, language-test coverage, multilingual
  intent coverage, web-TDZ ✓
- issue-386 Playwright spec: **9 passed** ✓

A `minor` changelog fragment is included; the version bump is driven by the
fragment (no manual `Cargo.toml` edit), so the version-modification guard stays
green.
title: Make seed data self-describing across the codebase; fix "Отмени сортировку" via inverse derivation (#386)

body:
## Summary
- Fixes #145 by generalizing feature capability/status questions beyond web search to supported capabilities such as arithmetic, translation, memory, configuration, diagnostics, agent mode, and definition fusion.
- Answers "yes" only when runtime-gated features are actually available in the current configuration; unavailable diagnostics, agent mode, definition fusion, offline/no-provider web search report "no" with the reason.
- Adds browser message commands for existing settings/actions, including diagnostics, demo/agent mode, theme/language/skin/chat/composer preferences, location, sidebar/deleted-conversation visibility, attachments, report issue, and memory actions.
- Keeps concrete action prompts routed to their primary handlers, e.g. `Can you calculate 2 + 2?` remains a calculation instead of a capability answer.
- Merged current `origin/main` into the PR branch and hardened E2E manual-mode helpers against the i18n hydration race introduced on the updated base.

## Reproduction
Ask `Ты можешь искать в интернете?`, `Can you do arithmetic?`, or `Can you show diagnostics?`.

Before this PR, only the original web-search wording was handled and the broader feature/configuration prompts could fall through or report too generically. After this PR, they return localized capability responses with runtime-aware availability. For configuration, send a command such as `Turn on diagnostics` or `Switch to dark theme` and the UI setting updates from chat.

## Tests
- `npm ci --prefix tests/e2e`

## Summary

Closes [#185](https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/issues/185).

Replaces the unknown-fallback for proof-shaped prompts (`Prove …`, `Show that …`, `Докажи …`, `साबित कर …`, `证明 …`) with a real universal proof / disproof engine that **never refuses**, and makes the rendering **configuration-aware** so the two `SolverConfig` sliders directly steer the answer style.

### Configuration-aware rendering (addresses [comment 4510303127](https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/pull/199#issuecomment-4510303127))

| Slider | Default | Behavior at high values | Threshold |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `guess_probability` | `0.8` | Prepends a localized "How I interpreted the request" header that names the formal system; for `PartialPlan` outcomes inserts two extra steps — a closed-sentence translation `⟦φ⟧ = ∀x. P(x) → Q(x)` (Definition) and a `relative-meta-logic` verification step (Inference). | `≥ 0.6` |
| `follow_up_probability` (new) | `0.75` | Appends a localized "Clarifying questions" footer for `Disproven`, `PartialPlan`, and `Inconclusive` arms enumerating the axiom set, definition, and acceptance criterion the user still has to confirm. | `≥ 0.5` |

Both sliders are reachable from the web preferences UI, the Rust `SolverConfig` fields, and the environment (`FORMAL_AI_GUESS_PROBABILITY`, `FORMAL_AI_FOLLOW_UP_PROBABILITY`). Every render decision is auditable on the append-only log as `policy:proof_guess_probability`, `policy:proof_follow_up_probability`, `proof_render:interpretation`, and `proof_render:follow_ups`.

### Reproduction prompt from #185

Before:

```
A (intent: unknown): I cannot answer that from local Links Notation rules yet. …
```

After (default sliders):

```
A (intent: proof_request): How I interpreted the request: …

Proof plan (method: axiom-set reduction).

1. Hypothesis: Reduce "determinism" to a checkable arithmetical / dynamical
   statement: pick an axiom set A and the precise reading of determinism
   inside A.
2. Inference: Apply Picard–Lindelöf inside Newtonian mechanics …
3. Definition: Translate the claim into a closed sentence φ in the chosen
   axiom set (PA, ZFC or Newtonian mechanics). The translation matches
   the canonical relative-meta-logic encoding ⟦φ⟧ = ∀x. P(x) → Q(x) …
4. Inference: Hand ⟦φ⟧ to the relative-meta-logic library: it runs the
   selected tactic (rewrite / induction / contradiction) and returns
   either a signed proof certificate or a counterexample. …
5. Conclusion: …

Clarifying questions:
- Which axiom set should we work in (PA, ZFC, Newtonian mechanics, …)?
- How exactly should "determinism" be defined inside that axiom set?
- What is the acceptance criterion (Gödel-style limit ok or not)?
```

## What's in this PR

### `src/proof_engine/` (new module)

| File | Responsibility |
| --- | --- |
| `types.rs` | `ProofMethod`, `StepKind`, `ProofStep`, `Proof`, `ProofOutcome`, **`ProofRenderConfig`**. All carry multilingual labels. |
| `arithmetic.rs` | Recognises `<expr> ⟨op⟩ <expr>` (=, ≠, <, >, ≤, ≥), normalises Unicode operators, evaluates both sides with the exact arbitrary-precision calculator in `crate::arithmetic`, emits `Proven` or `Disproven`. |
| `library.rs` | Multilingual library of classical theorems: Pythagorean theorem (construction), Euclid's infinitude of primes (contradiction), √2 irrationality (contradiction), Fermat's little theorem (induction), Gödel's first incompleteness theorem (known-theorem citation), Laplacian determinism (axiom-set reduction). |
| `mod.rs` | `attempt_proof` and **`attempt_proof_with_config`**. Routes arithmetic → library lookup → Gödel + determinism combo → generic partial plan. Never refuses. High guess expands the plan with the deep-reasoning steps. |
| `presenter.rs` | `render_outcome` and **`render_outcome_with_config`**. Localizes headings (`Proof` / `Доказательство` / `प्रमाण` / `证明`), method labels, step labels, missing-inputs section, the new "Interpretation" header, and the new "Clarifying questions" footer. |

### Handler integration

`src/solver_handlers/user_intent.rs::try_proof_request_with_config` calls the engine, emits the `policy:*` / `proof_render:*` events, and hands the rendered body to `finalize_simple`. Confidence: `Proven`/`Disproven` → 0.85, `PartialPlan` → 0.6, `Inconclusive` → 0.4. `src/solver.rs::handle_specialized_pattern` builds a `ProofRenderConfig` from the live `SolverConfig` once per iteration and dispatches the proof-request handler through the config-aware entry point.

### Tests

* 13 baseline tests in `tests/unit/proof_request.rs` (intent routing, multilingual proofs, no-refusal contract).
* **16 new tests** in `tests/unit/proof_request_config.rs` covering the high-guess / low-follow-up axis, deep-reasoning step injection, multilingual interpretation/question labels, log-event emission, env overrides, and the `ProofRenderConfig` threshold helpers.
* Plus the existing in-module unit tests inside `src/proof_engine/*`.

Full suite: **604 passed, 0 failed, 69 ignored** (160 + 11 + 433).

### Docs

- `docs/case-studies/issue-185/README.md` extended with the configuration-aware rendering section, the new requirements (R185-14 .. R185-17), and the 16 new test descriptions.
- `changelog.d/20260520_200000_issue_185_proof_request.md` updated to describe both the universal prover and the new sliders.

## Test plan

- [x] `cargo fmt --all` — clean
- [x] `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings` — clean
- [x] `cargo test --all` — 604 passed, 0 failed, 69 ignored
- [x] `rust-script scripts/check-file-size.rs` — no new files near the 1000-line cap
- [ ] CI checks pass on remote
- [ ] Reproduction prompt verified end-to-end against the deployed surface (not possible locally; rely on the test suite)

Refs: #185

Fixes #185
title: Universal proof/disproof engine for proof requests (issue #185)

Fixes #14

## Summary

This PR delivers the **unified-surface** vision from issue #14: every interface — the GitHub Pages demo, Telegram bot, CLI, library, and HTTP server — now routes through the same universal 11-step solver pipeline. The demo's hardcoded prompt→answer table has been replaced with live solver handlers that mirror `src/solver.rs`.

### Unified demo worker (`docs/demo/formal_ai_worker.js`)
- Rewrites the worker from a lookup-table (`answers[code]`) to a `solve(prompt, history)` function that runs the same handler cascade as the Rust solver: greeting → identity → conversation memory → arithmetic → JavaScript execution → concept lookup → hello-world → unknown fallback.
- Every answer carries `intent`, `confidence`, and `evidence` arrays so the diagnostics panel works identically across surfaces.
- Hello-world programs for non-JavaScript languages now honestly report that the browser sandbox cannot invoke the toolchain instead of faking execution status.

### Arithmetic evaluator (`src/arithmetic.rs`, mirrored in worker)
- Precedence-climbing parser (primary → unary → multiplicative → additive) handles symbolic operators (`+`, `-`, `*`, `/`, `%`, `×`, `÷`), English-word operators (`plus`, `minus`, `times`, `divided by`, `modulo`), parentheses, decimals, negative numbers, and thousand separators.
- Division-by-zero and overflow produce clean error messages instead of panics.

### Offline concept knowledge base (`src/concepts.rs`, `data/seed/concepts.lino`)
- 9 seeded records: universal solver, event log, Links Notation, doublet, Wikipedia, Wikidata, Wiktionary, WebAssembly, Rust.
- `extract_concept_term` parses "what is X?", "tell me about X", "define X", "explain X", etc.
- `lookup_concept` matches by term, alias, or slug with case-insensitive normalization and article stripping.
- Every answer cites its source and source kind.

### JavaScript execution handler
- In the Rust solver (`try_javascript_execution`): honestly reports that the Rust runtime has no JS engine and advises running the snippet in a browser or Node.js.
- In the demo worker (`tryJavaScriptExecution`): actually executes the snippet in a sandboxed `new Function` call, captures `console.log` output, and reports errors.

### Multi-turn conversation memory (`ConversationTurn`, `ConversationRole`)
- `solve_with_history` threads prior turns into the event log as `prior_turn:user` / `prior_turn:assistant` events.
- Handlers: `try_recall_name` (scans history for "My name is X"), `try_recall_last_question`, `try_summarize_conversation`.
- The demo passes `history` from the React state to the worker on every message.

### localStorage persistence with Links Notation (`docs/demo/preferences.js`)
- `FormalAiPreferences.save()` encodes `{ demoMode, diagnosticsMode }` as a Links Notation doublet tree under the `formal-ai.preferences.v1` localStorage key.
- `FormalAiPreferences.load()` parses it back, with graceful fallback to defaults.
- `app.js` reads preferences on mount and writes on every toggle change.

### Module split refactor
- Extracted `src/arithmetic.rs` (335 lines): arithmetic evaluator types and functions.
- Extracted `src/concepts.rs` (220 lines): `ConceptRecord`, `CONCEPTS`, `extract_concept_term`, `lookup_concept`.
- Extracted `src/solver_handlers.rs` (626 lines): all `try_*` handler free functions and `finalize_simple`/`build_evidence_links`.
- `src/solver.rs` reduced from 1191 → 518 lines; `src/solver_helpers.rs` reduced from 1212 → 663 lines.
- All files now under the 1000-line cap enforced by `scripts/check-file-size.rs`.

### Testing
- `tests/unit/mvp/reasoning_paths.rs` (273 lines): arithmetic evaluation (operators, precedence, division-by-zero, English words), concept lookup (term, alias, unknown, citation), JavaScript execution detection, conversation history (name recall, previous question, summary).
- `examples/universal_solver_tour.rs`: end-to-end walkthrough of JS execution, source refresh, translation, and multi-turn conversation recall.
- `examples/try_arithmetic.rs` + `experiments/try_arithmetic.rs`: quick arithmetic evaluator smoke tests.

### Documentation
- `docs/REQUIREMENTS.md`: R83–R89 covering unified demo, localStorage persistence, arithmetic, concept lookup, conversation memory, JS execution, and case study.
- `docs/case-studies/issue-14/README.md`: full case study with raw data, online research, requirement analysis, solution plan, and verification evidence.
- `changelog.d/20260515_120000_issue_14_unified_demo.md`: changelog fragment for release.

## Verification

- `cargo fmt --all -- --check` ✓
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings` ✓
- `cargo test --all-targets` — 171 passing (31 lib + 2 integration + 138 unit), 78 ignored, 0 failed ✓
- `cargo test --doc` ✓
- `rust-script scripts/check-file-size.rs` — all files within limits ✓
- `cargo run --example universal_solver_tour` — JS handler, source refresh, translation, multi-turn recall all produce correct output ✓

## Requirements Coverage

| ID | Requirement | Where |
| --- | --- | --- |
| R83 | Route every demo answer through the universal solver — no hardcoded table | `docs/demo/formal_ai_worker.js` rewritten with `solve()` pipeline |
| R84 | Persist demo/diagnostics toggles in localStorage using Links Notation | `docs/demo/preferences.js` + `app.js` integration |
| R85 | Solve arithmetic prompts with symbols, English words, parens, decimals, modulo | `src/arithmetic.rs` + `try_arithmetic` handler + worker mirror |
| R86 | Answer "what is X?" from seeded Wikipedia/Wikidata/Wiktionary data with source citation | `src/concepts.rs` + `data/seed/concepts.lino` + `try_concept_lookup` |
| R87 | Remember conversation: recall name, previous question, summarize prior turns | `ConversationTurn`/`solve_with_history` + `try_conversation_memory` |
| R88 | Declare JS execution capability — Rust explains no runtime, browser actually executes | `try_javascript_execution` in solver + worker's `tryJavaScriptExecution` |
| R89 | Compile case-study evidence under `docs/case-studies/issue-14/` | `README.md` + `raw-data/` with JSON evidence |

## Summary

Resolves the issue #410 program of work in two parts:

1. **Dependency refresh** (responding to the latest review request — "use the latest versions web-search, web-capture, only stable latest Rust, and all other dependencies should be fully updated to the latest ones").
2. **Refreshed case study** documenting that the upstream `web-search`/`web-capture` readiness blockers are now resolved.

Everything is kept in this single PR.

## Dependency updates

- **Rust toolchain / MSRV:** `rust-version` raised to **1.96** (current stable) and the Docker builder image bumped to `rust:1.96-slim`. This matches the `web-search` and `web-capture` crate MSRVs (both `1.96`), removing the previous MSRV-mismatch blocker.
- **Rust crates:** `clap` 4.6, `doublets` 0.4.0, `link-calculator` 0.19.0, `meta-language` 0.45 (+ transitive updates via `cargo update`).
- **Web bundle:** `react`/`react-dom` **19.2.7**, `marked` **18.0.5**, `dompurify` **3.4.10**; `src/web/vendor.bundle.js` rebuilt; Bun pinned to `1.3.14`.
- **Desktop:** `electron` 42, `electron-builder` 26. **VS Code:** `@vscode/test-web` 0.0.80, `@vscode/vsce` 3.9.2.

### React 19 regression fixed

React 19 compares `dangerouslySetInnerHTML` by **object identity** (React 18 compared the inner `__html` string). A fresh markdown-HTML object on every render therefore re-assigned `innerHTML` each pass, wiping the out-of-band `.code-block` enhancements (syntax highlighting + copy buttons) grafted in by `enhanceCodeBlocks`. Fixed by memoizing the rendered markdown object by `message.content` in `src/web/app.js`, so the object identity stays stable while the text is unchanged.

### Latest-stable lint compliance

The repo's CI runs `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features` with `RUSTFLAGS: -Dwarnings`, and `Cargo.toml` enables the `nursery`/`pedantic` lint groups. The latest stable toolchain (clippy `0.1.96`) reports 32 lints on pre-existing code that older clippy releases did not (`missing_const_for_fn`, `unnecessary_map_or`, `manual_repeat_n`, `manual_midpoint`, `manual_is_multiple_of`). Applied clippy's machine-suggested fixes — `const fn` where derivable, `Option::is_none_or`, `std::iter::repeat_n`, `f64::midpoint`, `u*::is_multiple_of` — so the build stays clean under `-Dwarnings` on the latest stable Rust required by this issue. All changes are behavior-preserving (verified by the full test suite).

## Case study refresh (`docs/case-studies/issue-410/`)

All three upstream blockers filed during the first pass are now **closed/resolved**:

- `web-search` is **published**: npm `@link-assistant/web-search@0.10.3`, crates.io `web-search@0.3.1` (`rust-version = 1.96`). (web-search #6, closed)
- `web-search` provider registry is now a **superset** of FormalAI's (40 IDs vs 32) and its default plan matches FormalAI's live defaults exactly. (web-search #5, closed)
- `web-capture` is at npm `1.10.9` / crates.io `0.3.31` with `rust-version = 1.96`; integration contract documented. (web-capture #135, closed)

The case study records that upstream readiness is reached and recommends the FormalAI-side configuration-gated adapter + provider-parity tests as the next, separately-tested step before any production default change.

## Verification

- `cargo fmt --check`: clean.
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features` under `RUSTFLAGS: -Dwarnings`: **exit 0** (the 32 latest-stable clippy findings above are resolved).
- `cargo test --all-features`: **passing** (989 unit tests). Updated the `docker_runtime` Dockerfile-version assertion to `1.96`; added a multilingual test pinning the `questioning_rigor` clarify margin across **en, ru, hi, zh**.
- `npm run --prefix tests/e2e check:language-test-coverage`: **OK** for en, ru, hi, zh (the dependency-refresh diff touches language-facing files, so the new multilingual test satisfies the coverage guard).
- `bun run build:web` → bundle reproducible (`git diff --exit-code` clean).
- Playwright e2e: **285 passed**. The 5 `issue-221` failures are a pre-existing **local** translation-data gap (the app emits its "could not translate … with the available formalization data" fallback); they fail identically on the React-18 baseline and pass in CI, which provides the data.
- VS Code tests: 50 pass. Desktop tests: 14 pass.
- All required CI jobs green (Lint, Test, Coverage, E2E local, Build, Version Check, Changelog).
- `main` merged into the branch; package version untouched (carried from `main`).

## How to reproduce the regression test

Before the fix, on React 19 a re-render of a chat message containing a fenced code block strips the `.code-block` wrapper/copy button. The Playwright suite (`tests/e2e`) exercises code-block rendering; the fix keeps those enhancements intact across re-renders.

Refs #410



Fixes #410
title: chore(#410): use latest web-search/web-capture, stable Rust 1.96, and refresh all dependencies

body:
Fixes the five UI/UX polish defects reported in #550 (mirrored upstream as
link-assistant/hive-mind#1963), **and** completes the strategic ask in full: a
total transition of the web app to **Chakra UI + modern JSX, styles in JavaScript,
built with the bun bundler** — all in this single PR, applied codebase-wide.

Closes #550. Supersedes link-assistant/hive-mind#1964 (its findings are incorporated
into the case study; that PR was opened against the wrong repo and can be closed).

A deep case study — full timeline, every requirement traced to its fix, per-problem
root causes, solution plans, and Chakra/library research — is compiled in
[`docs/case-studies/issue-550/`](https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/tree/issue-550-c636b0e4075d/docs/case-studies/issue-550).

---

## 1. The five UI/UX fixes

None is a fresh bug — each is a residual rough edge from features shipped quickly
across #488 (the thinking preview) and #541/#549 (the theme pass + desktop
auto-update panel). The thread connecting P4 and P5 is that `styles.css` had **zero
CSS custom properties**: light and dark were *manually duplicated hex* across three
layers, so any new surface or button silently missed rules the older ones had.

| # | Symptom | Root cause | Fix |
|---|---|---|---|
| **P1** | The reasoning fade repeats per step line, so two stacked steps show two separate gradients instead of one "more below" fade. | The `mask-image` gradient was on the per-line `.thinking-preview-previous`, not the scrolling container. | Move it to `.thinking-preview-collapsed:has(.thinking-preview-previous)` as one `linear-gradient(to bottom, transparent 0, #000 1.4em)`; drop the per-line mask. `:has()` keeps a lone first step unmasked. |
| **P2** | Thinking detail is clipped mid-sentence ("some parts are omitted"). | Detail was capped at **120 chars** in two parallel constants — `truncate_thinking_detail` (Rust) and `thinkingDetailText` (JS). | Raise the cap to **600** in both, kept in sync; a pathological over-cap detail still truncates with an ellipsis. |
| **P3** | A message showing only thinking renders a narrow ~116px body, then jumps to full width when the answer streams. | A leftover `.pending .message-body { width: 116px }` (+ dead `.typing::after`) pinned the pending body. | Remove the clamp so the pending body uses the settled width — no reflow. |
| **P4** | The desktop **services** / **update** panels render light surfaces in dark mode. | Only light rules were written when those panels were added; the dark counterparts were never hand-duplicated. | Add complete dark rules for every services/update surface in **both** `:root[data-theme="dark"]` **and** `@media (prefers-color-scheme: dark)`. |
| **P5** | Only some top-bar buttons react to hover — partial feedback. | Top-bar controls were styled piecemeal; newer buttons never got the older ones' `:hover` rules. | One shared hover + active-hover + `:focus-visible` ring for **every** top-bar control, light and dark. |

**"Fix it in all places."** Problem **2** is fixed in **both** runtimes (Rust core +
JS worker, cross-referenced to stay in sync); problems **4** and **5** are fixed in
**both** dark layers.

## 2. Full Chakra UI + JSX migration (M2/M3)

Per the maintainer's directive — *"My requirement is translation to Chakra, and using
modern JSX, with styles in JavaScript. Use bun bundler for building … we do it in this
PR … Nothing can be deferred or delayed to other pull requests"* — the web app is now
fully migrated:

* **JSX authoring + bun bundler.** The app source is now `src/web/app/main.jsx`,
  authored in modern JSX and compiled to `src/web/app.js` by the **bun bundler**
  (`build:web`). `tsconfig.json` pins bun's classic JSX runtime (`jsxFactory: h`,
  `h === React.createElement`).
* **Chakra runtime.** The app mounts under
  `createRoot(...).render(<ChakraProvider value={chakraSystem}><App /></ChakraProvider>)`;
  React, ReactDOM, `@chakra-ui/react`, and `@emotion/react` are bundled into `app.js`.
  UI renders through Chakra's `chakra.*` styled factory.
* **Styles in JavaScript via a token bridge.** `src/web/app/theme.js` bridges every
  `--fa-*` CSS design token 1:1 into a Chakra `semanticToken` (`fa.surface.card` →
  `var(--fa-surface-card)`), with `preflight: false` and `globalCss: {}` so Chakra
  emits no reset/global rules — `styles.css` stays the single source of truth for colour.
* **Reusable component.** A single `ToolbarButton` renders all 11 topbar controls
  through `chakra.*`, so shared markup/classes/a11y are guaranteed by construction.

### Retraction of the earlier "CSP-blocked" claim

An earlier revision of this PR argued the Chakra/Emotion runtime could not ship —
because (a) #479's `style-src 'self'` CSP forbids Emotion's runtime `<style>`
injection and (b) `app.js` is served un-transpiled. **Both premises were false:**

* The application page **`src/web/app/index.html` carries no CSP.** The `style-src
  'self'` policy is only on the *marketing* pages (`src/web/index.html`,
  `src/web/docs/index.html`, `src/web/download/index.html`), which never load `app.js`.
* `app.js` is **already bun-built** from `main.jsx` (`build:web`), gated by
  `git diff --exit-code` in `.github/workflows/release.yml`.

There was no architectural block; the migration ships in full.

### How it stays pixel-identical

The classic JSX runtime makes the JSX compile back to the *same* `h()` calls the file
used before. An AST codemod (`experiments/codemod-h-to-jsx.mjs`) performed the
conversion, and `experiments/verify-jsx-equivalence.mjs` proves equivalence: it
compiles `main.jsx` before/after with `bun build --packages external`, canonicalises
both via `@babel/generator {compact:true}`, and confirms the only differences are
three hand-converted, semantics-preserving residuals. Verified by the full Playwright
suite (**332 passed / 1 skipped**) and both static web guards.

---

## 3. Tests & verification

- **`tests/unit/issue_1963.rs`** (4 tests, en/ru/hi/zh) — pins the 600-char detail
  bound and proves the cap counts Unicode scalar values, not bytes.
- **`tests/e2e/tests/issue-1963.spec.js`** — behavioral regression for P1/P3/P4/P5
  (continuous container mask, pending-width parity, dark services/update colors,
  hover + focus-ring across every topbar control).
- `cargo test --test unit`, `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets
  --all-features` → clean.
- All web guards pass: `check:i18n`, `check:language-parity`,
  `check:language-test-coverage`, `check:intent-coverage`, `check:web-tdz`,
  `check:web-hardcoded-ui`, and the terminal-vocabulary mirror check.
- `git diff --exit-code` on `vendor.bundle.js` / `ocr.bundle.js` / `app.js` → clean
  after `bun run build:web`.
- Full Playwright local suite → **332 passed / 1 skipped**.

## 4. Changelog

`changelog.d/20260621_133148_issue_550_ui_ux_polish.md` (`bump: patch`).
title: fix(web): five UI/UX polish fixes + full Chakra UI / JSX migration (#550)

body:
## Summary

Implements **E1** of the #511 epic (visible fix): a terminal-command prompt no longer dead-ends in the worker's `unknown` fallback, and the binary agent toggle is replaced by a three-way **Chat / Agent / Full-Auto** mode radio.

Stacked on the parent branch `issue-511-26d6b8408464` (PR #512) — the full specification (R1–R20, solution plans, dependency graph) lives in `docs/case-studies/issue-511/`, which is delivered by that parent PR.

Delivers **R5** and **R6**; scaffolding for R1. Hermetic (no Docker, no real CLI, no subscriptions).

## R5 — Terminal-command intent (`tryTerminalCommand`), both engines

Added in parity to the JS worker (`src/web/formal_ai_worker.js`) and the Rust solver (new `src/solver_terminal.rs`, wired into `src/solver.rs` just before the `unknown` fallback). It detects shell-command shapes:

- fenced / backtick commands (`` `ls ~` ``),
- "run … in terminal" / «выполни … в терминале» phrasings (localized for en/ru/hi/zh),
- an explicit leading shell token (`ls`, `pwd`, `cat`, `git status`, …),

and returns an `agent_suggestion` response that (a) names the detected command, (b) explains Agent mode, and (c) offers to switch and grant the `shell` capability when agent mode is off. Detection is precise enough to avoid false positives (e.g. "run a marathon next year" → no match).

Because the repo enforces 4-language parity (`check-language-test-coverage`), detection, localized bodies, and tests cover **en / ru / hi / zh**. Chinese run-verbs are matched by substring (no word boundaries in CJK).

## R6 — Three-way mode radio

- Replaced the binary `agent-toggle` with a `chat` / `agent` / `full-auto` radiogroup in both the toolbar (`data-testid="mode-radio"`) and the drawer (`drawer-mode-radio`), with `role="radio"` / `aria-checked` options (`data-testid="mode-option-<mode>"`).
- New `mode` preference (`"chat" | "agent" | "fullAuto"`); the legacy `agentMode` boolean is derived (`mode !== "chat"`) for back-compat. `normalizeMode` upgrades a pre-#513 store that only had `agentMode "on"` to the `agent` radio.
- Topbar status label now reflects the active mode (`status.mode` → "Mode: {mode}", `data-testid="mode-status"`).
- Localized `buttons.fullAuto`, `titles.fullAuto`, `titles.modeGroup`, `status.mode` for all 4 locales.

## Reproduction (before this PR)

`Выполни \`ls ~\` в терминале` (ru) and `run \`ls ~\` in terminal` (en) both returned `unknown` ("That one is new to me"); the toolbar only had an off-by-default binary toggle.

## Tests

- **Rust unit** (`tests/unit/issue_513.rs`): en/ru/hi/zh terminal prompts resolve to `agent_suggestion` and name the command, not `unknown`.
- **JS worker**: covered via the multilingual intent suite + `experiments/issue-513-terminal-intent-parity.mjs` (19 parity checks, all engines/languages).
- **e2e** (`tests/e2e/tests/issue-513.spec.js`): en + ru terminal prompts suggest Agent mode (not the unknown fallback); the mode radio switches Chat → Agent → Full-Auto with `aria-checked` + status-label assertions.
- Migrated all existing specs that used the old `agent-toggle` selector to `mode-radio` / `mode-option-agent`.

## Verification (local)

- `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features` (clean), `cargo test --all-features` (all pass).
- All 5 JS guards: `check:i18n`, `check:language-test-coverage`, `check:language-parity`, `check:intent-coverage`, `check:web-tdz`.
- e2e: issue-513 + issue-337 + multilingual + 6 migrated specs all green.
- File-size guard: largest touched `.rs` (`src/solver_terminal.rs`, 218 lines) well under the 1000-line cap.

Closes #513.


---

## Review follow-up: de-hardcode natural language (no hardcoded NL, enforced by CI)

Per maintainer review, the terminal intent now sources **all** natural language from seed data, with CI enforcement and docs so the pattern can't regress:

- **Triggers → seed.** Terminal/shell phrases, run verbs, Chinese run verbs, and leading shell tokens moved to `data/seed/terminal-commands.lino`; parsed in Rust via `src/seed/terminal_commands.rs` (`seed::terminal_command_vocabulary()`), mirrored byte-identically in the JS worker.
- **Responses → seed.** Four-language answers live in `data/seed/multilingual-responses.lino` (`agent_suggestion` / `agent_suggestion_active`, `{command}` placeholder); both engines look them up.
- **Total reference-closure at zero.** Every new value token is grounded as a first-class meaning via `scripts/close-total.py` → `data/seed/closure-generated-*.lino` (idempotent; `audit-total-closure.py` reports `unresolved_distinct: 0`). This was the real CI red on earlier commits.
- **CI gates.** New step *“Check terminal vocabulary worker mirror is in sync with seed”* (`issue-513-sync-worker-terminal.mjs --check`) + the existing `total_closure` gate (`cargo test --tests`).
- **Docs.** New `docs/design/no-hardcoded-natural-language.md` (meanings ↔ naturalization model + worked example); `CONTRIBUTING.md` convention #2 upgraded to the full no-hardcoded-NL rule.

## Review follow-up: E2E timeouts

Added reasonable **per-test**, **global (whole-suite)**, **assertion**, and **navigation/action** timeouts to both `playwright.local.config.js` and `playwright.pages.config.js` so a hung worker/server/deployment aborts promptly. (The `issue-221.spec.js` failures that appear in a network-isolated sandbox are pre-existing and pass in CI, where the live Wiktionary fallback is reachable; `main` E2E is green.)
title: E1: Terminal-command intent + three-way Mode radio (visible fix for #511)

body:
Fixes #12

## Summary

This PR delivers both the **vision synthesis** for issue #12 and the first concrete **universal solver** implementation that turns that vision into running code. Telegram, the OpenAI-compatible REST surface, and the Rust library now share the same 11-step reasoning pipeline instead of dispatching to bespoke intent helpers.

### Documents
- Root `VISION.md`, `GOALS.md`, `NON-GOALS.md` for the link-native associative direction, extended with the universal solver model.
- `docs/case-studies/issue-12` (raw issue/PR evidence, external research, holistic requirements, root-cause analysis, solution plan, boundaries).
- `README.md` + `docs/REQUIREMENTS.md` updated through issue #12.

### Universal 11-step solver loop
Every reply now walks the same pipeline regardless of intent: **impulse → formalization → context → history → decomposition → TDD → synthesis → combination → verification → simplification → documentation**. The loop is implemented in `src/solver.rs` / `src/solver_helpers.rs` and wired into `FormalAiEngine.answer` so library, HTTP, and Telegram callers all share it.

### Append-only event log
`src/event_log.rs` records each step with content-addressed FNV-1a 64-bit ids. Steps surface in `evidence_links` and in a Links Notation `steps:` trace block; HTTP/Telegram replies advertise the trace pointer so callers can drill in.

### Knowledge graph projection
`GraphNode` / `GraphEdge` / `KnowledgeGraph` and a new `/v1/graph` endpoint serve the graph in Links Notation, JSON, or Graphviz DOT.

### Multilingual impulse tagging
Unicode-block based language detection (`Language::{English, Russian, Hindi, Chinese, Unknown}`) tags every impulse with `language:<slug>` evidence.

### OpenAI surface upgrades
- `rate_limit` metadata on `/v1/models`
- SSE streaming for `/v1/chat/completions` when `stream: true`
- `SolverConfig` (offline mode, autonomy bounds) driven by environment variables

### Telegram trace pointers
Every reply ends with a `/trace <id>` footer so users can request the inline event log for the message they just received.

## TDD MVP test suite
`tests/unit/mvp/` pins down every current and future MVP feature drawn from `VISION.md`, `GOALS.md`, `NON-GOALS.md`, and `docs/REQUIREMENTS.md` (R55–R71). Tests that the universal solver now satisfies have been un-ignored; remaining `#[ignore = "MVP-target: ..."]` tests still document the road ahead without blocking CI. Run all of them with `cargo test -- --include-ignored`.

| Module | Surface or invariant pinned down |
| --- | --- |
| `chat_surface` | bounded chat, identity, greeting, diagnostics defaults |
| `code_generation` | top programming languages, execution evidence, unsupported-execution honesty |
| `multilingual` | English / Russian / Hindi / Chinese baseline conversations |
| `openai_compatibility` | Chat Completions, Responses, HTTP routes, streaming, rate-limit metadata |
| `telegram_surface` | private / public chats, code formatting, message chunking, trace footer |
| `links_network` | doublet links, dynamic types, add-only history, concept uniqueness, trace links |
| `reasoning_loop` | impulse → local → external → decomposition → candidates → validation → smallest sufficient answer |
| `source_cache` | external source caching with provenance and TTL |
| `agent_isolation` | chat-vs-agent autonomy boundary and isolated execution |
| `translation_via_links` | Links Notation as the language of meaning |
| `network_visualization` | optional link-graph view alongside chat |
| `transparent_state` | querying the network through chat without leaking internal state by default |

## Verification
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings`
- `cargo test --all-features --verbose`
- `cargo test --doc --verbose`
- `cargo test -- --include-ignored` (runs MVP-target expectations; remaining ignored tests document not-yet-implemented behavior)
- `rust-script scripts/check-file-size.rs`
- `rust-script scripts/check-changelog-fragment.rs`
- `git diff --check`
- `npm --prefix tests/e2e run test:local`

## Sources
External sources are cited in `docs/case-studies/issue-12/README.md`, including Deep.Foundation, Link Foundation tools, doublets-rs, Wikidata/Wikifunctions/Rosetta Code, WebVM/CheerpX, and link-assistant/agent.
title: Issue 12: vision synthesis + universal 11-step solver
