body:
## What This PR Does

Closes #244.

This PR carries the Issue #244 **vision-tracking and planning** work. Across six audit passes it progressively planned, re-scoped, and closed the issue batches needed to reach the deterministic, link-native universal problem solver the vision describes — and keeps every planning document in sync with the actual code state.

While this PR was in flight, the same final parity work it had planned (E33–E34) was **independently implemented and merged to `main`** via PRs #328 and #329. After re-merging `main` (now at **v0.149.0**), the net change of this PR relative to `main` is the **issue #244 case-study corpus plus the cross-document sync** that records the post-merge state. No Rust/JS source diff remains versus `main` — the parity code is already there; this PR makes the docs tell the truth about it.

### Vision-completeness conclusion (sixth-pass audit, 2026-05-29)

Every planned epic toward the vision is now closed and merged:

| Batch | Issues | Status |
| --- | --- | --- |
| E1-E14 (foundation) | #246-#259 | Closed / merged |
| E15-E20 (follow-ups) | #278-#283 | Closed / merged |
| E21-E27 (reasoning) | #298-#304 | Closed / merged (PRs #305-#311) |
| E28-E32 (synthesis) | #313-#317 | Closed / merged (PRs #319-#323) |
| E33-E34 (parity) | #326-#327 | Closed / merged (PRs #328-#329) |

**With E1-E34 all merged, no vision-planning epic remains open for issue #244.**

### What the parity batch (E33-E34) delivered (now on `main`)

- **E33 — universal multilingual operation vocabulary (#326, PR #328).** The text-manipulation handler no longer matches hardcoded English literals. Every operation is recognised by canonicalising the prompt against one shared seed table, `data/seed/operation-vocabulary.lino`, which lists each operation's surface forms in all four supported languages (`en|ru|hi|zh`). The Rust core loads it via `seed::operation_vocabulary()`; the browser worker loads the same file via `src/web/seed_loader.js`. Adding a surface form — or a whole language — is a **data edit, never a code change**.
- **E34 — cross-runtime parity (#327, PR #329).** The JavaScript browser worker (`src/web/formal_ai_worker.js`) routes synthesis prompts through `tryLinkNativeSynthesis`, `tryProgramSynthesis`, and `tryTextManipulation`, deriving the same synthesis/numeric/program/text answers as the Rust core. Parity is pinned by the shared fixture `data/parity/cross-runtime-synthesis.json`, the Rust test `shared_cross_runtime_synthesis_fixture_matches_rust_solver`, and the browser e2e `tests/e2e/tests/issue-327.spec.js`.

The synthesis step remains **general**: the universal 11-step loop derives answers by composing decomposed sub-results over the links network instead of resolving from seeded handlers, and the imported industry benchmark suite passes **10/10** with a `minimum_pass_count` ratchet — without per-case memorization.

### Docs synced in this PR

- **`ROADMAP.md`** — header sixth-pass note; the fifth-pass parity-gap narration reworded so it reads as subsequently closed; the parity batch table marks E33/E34 closed/merged.
- **`REQUIREMENTS.md`** — intro and the R250/R255 rows mark E33-E34 closed (PRs #328-#329); no vision-planning epic remains open. (Main's #327 section keeps R246-R249; the #244 planning section was renumbered to R250-R255 to avoid the ID collision.)
- **`ARCHITECTURE.md` §16/§17** — the parity batch rewritten as closed, with verified code anchors (`seed::operation_vocabulary()`, `src/web/seed_loader.js`, the three `try*` routing functions, the cross-runtime fixture and tests).
- **`VISION.md`** — parity gap marked closed; E33-E34 added to the merged-batches list.
- **`docs/case-studies/issue-244/`** — `README.md` and `proposed-issues.md` get timeline rows, status tables, and closed/merged headers for E33-E34, plus the full case-study corpus (raw data, online research, proposed issue bodies).

All referenced code anchors were verified present in the merged tree before committing.

### Verification

- Branch merged up to date with `main` (v0.149.0); merge status **CLEAN / MERGEABLE**.
- Post-merge: `cargo build`, `cargo test` (673 passed, 0 failed), `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy` all green.
- `tests/e2e/scripts/check-language-test-coverage.mjs` — passes (text-manipulation handler is byte-identical to `main`).
- No manual version edit (the `version-check` CI guard forbids it; releases are automated via changelog fragments on push to `main`). This PR is docs-only relative to `main`, so no changelog fragment is required.
title: docs: issue #244 vision tracking — sixth-pass audit records E1-E34 all merged (parity batch closed)

body:
## Summary
- Rework JSON -> LiNo cache projection to drop generated `at-NNNN` array ids and scalar `string` tags, collapse Wikidata language/value maps, and keep ordered scalar arrays as native parenthesized value lists.
- Move Wikidata cache snapshots into typed `entity/`, `property/`, and `lexeme/` folders with raw `.json` beside every `.lino`; move the Wiktionary reference cache to `data/cache/wiktionary/en/reference.{json,lino}`.
- Validate all checked-in `data/**/*.lino` with the real parser as-is, normalize seed/benchmark files to remove blank/comment-only lines, and add recursive Wikidata grounding closure coverage.
- Keep benchmark fixtures raw-parseable while preserving record parsing by splitting records on top-level entries instead of blank lines.
- Force LF checkout for `.lino` and cache `.json` files so canonical cache equality remains stable on Windows runners.
- **Restore human-readable seed scalars:** replace the codepoint byte-dump encoding with quoted scalars, teach every LiNo parser to decode them, and add CI guards to keep the seed legible.

## Review response (codepoint byte-dumps)
Addresses https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/pull/399#issuecomment-4660584608.

Commit `b1e1bc6` had fixed parse-validity the wrong way: readable strings were replaced with raw codepoint byte-dumps (e.g. `answer codepoints 72 105 ...` spelling "Hi, how may I help you?"). This round fixes **Defect 1**:

- All 57 `data/seed/*.lino` files converted from byte-dumps back to readable quoted scalars (`text "Hi, how may I help you?"`). Zero byte-dumps remain.
- Each quoted value picks a delimiter (`"`, `'`, or backtick) absent from its own text, so the inner quote never needs escaping (works around a canonical-parser quirk) while preserving the surface text verbatim.
- Space-significant translation markers stay quoted to preserve leading/trailing whitespace (`text "translate … to "`, `text " का "`, `text " में अनुवाद"`).
- All five parsers decode the three delimiters in sync: Rust runtime (`src/seed/parser.rs`), web runtime (`src/web/seed_loader.js`), e2e parser (`tests/e2e/scripts/lino-seed-parser.mjs`), the embedded worker fallback in `src/web/formal_ai_worker.js`, and the hand-maintained `tests/source` mirror.
- `surface_text` (`src/seed/meanings.rs`) reads the readable `text` child first, falling back to `codepoints` only for legacy data.

### CI guards added
- **Rule 1 — no codepoint byte-dumps in seed:** `tests/unit/data_files.rs::seed_lino_files_have_no_codepoint_byte_dumps` fails the build on any reintroduced `codepoints`/`unformalized-raw` byte-dump (bare integer runs outside quoted spans).
- **Rule 7 — no inline unit tests in `src/`:** `tests/unit/ci-cd/source_test_placement.rs::src_has_no_inline_unit_tests` walks `src/**/*.rs` and fails on any real `#[test]`/`#[cfg(test)]`/`mod tests` (string-literal occurrences ignored). Tests belong under `tests/`.

### Deferred to follow-up issues
These review items are intentionally out of scope for this round and documented in `docs/case-studies/issue-398/README.md`:
- **Defect 3 / rule 4:** own slugs as hyphenated full English words (`links-root`, not `links_root`); external `Q…/L…/P…` ids stay as-is.
- **Defect 4 / rule 5:** full recursive grounding closure for every core meaning.
- **Rule 6:** no hardcoded domain-data string literals in `src/`.

## Review response (pipe-packed multi-values)
Addresses **Defect 4 / rule 5** of https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/pull/399#issuecomment-4660584608 — multi-values must be real links `(a b c)`, never in-string separators like `"a|b|c"`.

- Migrated every pipe-packed field in `data/seed/*.lino` to the canonical reference-list form `keyword ("a" "b c" d)` (`supported_languages`, `tasks`, `languages`, `inputs`, `outputs`, aliases, …). `grep` confirms no `|` remains in any seed value outside the exempt `code` field.
- `code` listings (Rust closures `|x|`, shell pipes) stay verbatim and are the sole exemption.
- Taught all four LiNo parsers to tokenize quoted scalars that may contain spaces: Rust `src/seed/parser.rs` (`split_reference_tokens`), web runtime `src/web/seed_loader.js`, e2e `tests/e2e/scripts/lino-seed-parser.mjs` (`splitReferenceList`), and the migration tooling.
- Added `formal_ai::supported_languages()` accessor (reads the `agent-info.lino` reference list) and routed every former ad-hoc `split('|')` consumer in the test suite through it.
- The data mutation is reproducible by algorithm: `scripts/migrate-pipe-lists.rs` (std-only) performs the rewrite.

### CI guard added
- **No pipe-packed multi-values in seed:** `tests/unit/data_files.rs::seed_lino_values_never_pipe_pack_multi_values` fails on *any* `|` in a seed value except the exempt `code` field, so pipe packing can never silently return.

## Review response (canonical cache)
Addresses https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/pull/399#issuecomment-4652626289:
- No `at-NNNN` generated array ids or scalar `string` tags remain in `data/**/*.lino`.
- Wikidata raw JSON lives beside compact LiNo in typed folders, and tests assert each LiNo projection is regenerated from the raw JSON snapshot.
- `links_notation::parse_lino(content.trim())` now validates data files without comment-stripping.
- `key: # ...` comment-only seed declarations and blank LiNo lines were removed.
- Recursive closure coverage walks bare Q/L/P ids found in seed/source and cache records, requiring checked-in `.json`/`.lino` for every reachable id.

## CI follow-up
Run https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/actions/runs/27166268807 failed only on `windows-latest` because Git checked `.lino` files out as CRLF, while `json_cache_file()` emits LF. Commit `b1e1bc60` adds `.gitattributes` so Windows checks out LiNo/cache snapshots with LF and the canonical equality test compares the intended bytes.

## Review response (empty-redefinition fields, overrides layer, whole-tree CI)
Addresses https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/pull/399#issuecomment-4663407299. Standards are recorded in `REQUIREMENTS.md` (R278-R283) under the governance rule **latest requirement overrides any earlier one**, each CI check mapped to a requirement.

**Defect 1 — empty-redefinition fields (resolved, R278).** Every `facet <kind>` wrapper whose child was an empty colon redefinition (`word_surface:`, `lexical_sense:`, …) is collapsed into the native Links Notation `subject predicate` form (`notation word_surface`, `denotation lexical_sense`) across the whole `data/seed` tree by the re-runnable `scripts/migrate-empty-facet-fields.rs`, which also regenerates the browser worker embed. `src/seed/meanings.rs` now reads both forms and de-duplicates targets. The previously-narrow guard is broadened: the tree-walking `seed_lino_files_have_no_empty_redefinition_fields` (`tests/unit/data_files.rs`) fails on any empty-bodied colon field with no deeper-indented child, with no hard-coded filename. Zero remain.

**New requirement — `data/overrides/` layer (resolved, R279).** A grounding override layer now sits beside `data/cache/` with the same per-id structure (`data/overrides/wikidata/{entity,property,lexeme}/<id>.lino`). Resolution is `(cache or live API) then overrides`: `formal_ai::seed::resolve` decorates a cached record with an override's facts (override wins on conflict, missing keys appended, untouched sections preserved). Each override records **why** it exists in a `reason "..."` line. The tree-walking `tests/unit/overrides.rs` fails when an override references an id with no checked-in cache record, omits its reason, carries no facts, or is **redundant** — repeating a value the cache already holds — so overrides shrink to zero as upstream catches up (CI check #6). The seeded example supplies the Hindi (`hi`) label Wikidata is missing for the KISS principle (Q131560).

**Whole-tree sweep + migration-first (resolved, R280/R281).** Both new guards walk their entire trees and assert on relative paths; both mutations are reproducible by algorithm rather than hand-edited (CI checks #1, #6, #7).

**Grounding floor (partial, R282).** Source-grounded meanings carry `grounded-in <Qid>` links whose recursive closure is verified against checked-in cache records. Grounding every remaining meaning to an external source — and sourcing per-language surfaces from those records instead of hand-typing them (CI checks #2, #3) — is a corpus-import effort larger than one PR and remains the primary follow-up; the grounding-floor CI check is held back until the data is grounded so it protects an invariant rather than only reddening CI. The override layer is the sanctioned interim way to record a source gap. Tracked in `docs/case-studies/issue-398/README.md`. The underscore→hyphen slug rename (CI check #5) and long-lived-quote sweep (CI check #4) are likewise tracked there: both are tree-wide renames whose string references reach into Rust tests and the worker embed, and are staged separately to avoid a destabilizing single-PR rewrite.

## Verification
- `git diff --check`
- `cargo fmt --all -- --check`
- `RUSTFLAGS=-Dwarnings cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features`
- `cargo test` (363 source-mirror + 798 unit/integration pass, 1 ignored)
- `cargo test --doc --verbose`
- `rust-script scripts/check-file-size.rs`
- `bun install --frozen-lockfile`
- `bun run build:web`
- `git diff --exit-code -- src/web/vendor.bundle.js src/web/ocr.bundle.js`
- `npm ci --prefix tests/e2e`
- `npm run --prefix tests/e2e check:i18n`
- `npm run --prefix tests/e2e check:language-parity`
- `npm run --prefix tests/e2e check:language-test-coverage`
- `npm run --prefix tests/e2e check:intent-coverage`
- `npm run --prefix tests/e2e check:web-tdz`

No screenshot attached because this is data/parser/cache behavior, not visual UI work.

Fixes #398




## Review response (full reference closure + role/predicate reconciliation)
Addresses points #1 and #2 of https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/pull/399#issuecomment-4668342875.

**Reconciled the "~96% undefined" measurement.** The audit recognized only colon-head `slug:` definitions and missed the *primary* nested-under-`meanings` definition form, undercounting definitions ~10×. The seed actually defines **478** meanings; `experiments/closure_audit.py` documents the measurement.

**#1 — full reference closure (the missing check, resolved).** Over genuine meaning-reference fields (`defined-by` + the four facets `notation`/`annotation`/`denotation`/`connotation`), **every** target resolves to a defined meaning — 0 dangling across all 35 `meanings*.lino` files. `tests/unit/data_files.rs::meaning_definition_references_resolve_to_defined_meanings` walks the whole lexicon and fails CI naming any reference that does not resolve (verified with a negative test). The flagged "core vocabulary" splits cleanly: `concept`/`relation`/`entity`/`action`/`noun`/`word_surface` **are** defined meanings; `number`/`phrase`/`greeting` were over-counts of `text "..."` surface strings (literal words), not meaning links.

**#2 — structural predicates vs meaning links (resolved).** The only undefined references in def-fields were **131 `role` predicate values**, previously undeclared. `data/seed/roles.lino` is now the single canonical registry of all **207** roles, each classified `kind meaning` (also a defined slug — 76) or `kind predicate` (role-only — 131). `scripts/generate-role-registry.py` regenerates it deterministically/idempotently; `every_role_value_is_declared_in_the_registry` and `role_registry_is_in_lockstep_with_usage` keep registry and usage in lockstep with correct kind classification.

### Deferred to follow-up (tracked in `docs/case-studies/issue-398/README.md`, row R398-14)
- **#3** reverse-dictionary / part-of-speech-as-meaning (the genuine residue: `verb`, `noun_phrase` used as POS tags but not yet defined meanings).
- **#4** multi-source `view` (WordNet cache, `data/view/`, `M-…` ids, `sources-registry.lino`, merge threshold).
- **#5** provable 1:1 meaning↔type via relative-meta-logic.
- **#6** minimal seed + on-demand expansion.


## Review response (total closure + multi-source view)
Addresses https://github.com/link-assistant/formal-ai/pull/399#issuecomment-4668929105. Standards recorded in `REQUIREMENTS.md` (R284-R288), latest-overrides-earlier, each CI check mapped to a requirement.

**Total closure widened from the backbone to every token (R284).** The earlier gate only proved the structured `defined-by`/facet/role backbone closed. This round drives *every* non-keyword, non-quoted value token in `data/seed/**.lino` to resolution. `scripts/audit-total-closure.py` is the single-source resolver (a token resolves to a defined meaning in either syntax, a grounded `Q…/L…/P…`/Wiktionary/WordNet id with a checked-in cache record, a declared role, or a language code); its `--json` mode reports **0 unresolved over 1,410 distinct tokens** (1,003 defined meanings). `scripts/close-total.py` is the idempotent migration that closed the gap — it defines each previously-dangling internal token as a first-class meaning (17 parent category concepts rooted at `concept`, plus 508 members parented by the category their predicate implies) into `data/seed/closure-generated-{01,02}.lino`. The backbone `reference_closure.rs` gate stays as a stricter subset — both hold.

**WordNet (OEWN 2024) ingested (R285).** `scripts/ground-wordnet.py` imports Open English WordNet 2024 offline (one download via the `wn` library, no per-word network calls) and caches **312** English lemmas as `.json` + lossless `.lino` under `data/cache/wordnet/en/`, recorded under CC BY 4.0.

**`data/view/` multi-source merge layer (R286).** `scripts/build-views.py` merges the WordNet and Wiktionary caches into **536** per-lemma view entities, each with a deterministic `M-<sha1[:12]>` id (`view:<lang>:<lemma>` → identical id on every run), a sorted `sources` list, and per-sense `provenance`. Senses sharing part-of-speech with gloss content-word Jaccard ≥ 0.5 merge and keep both sources; otherwise they stay separate. `--check` reruns the build in memory to detect drift, reconfirms id determinism, asserts no provenance-less field, and runs the merge-threshold self-tests. **14** entities are genuinely multi-source on real data (both WordNet and Wiktionary).

**`sources-registry.lino` (R287).** `data/seed/sources-registry.lino` enumerates every ingested source (Wikidata, Wiktionary, WordNet, Wikipedia) with its API endpoint, permissive license, and cache path.

### CI gates added (`tests/unit/total_closure.rs`)
Six gates that fail immediately — and name offenders, never a bare count — if: any seed value token is unresolved; the seed collapses below hundreds of meanings / a full token surface; the WordNet cache is absent; `sources-registry.lino` omits an ingested source / API / license, or a populated cache dir is unregistered; `data/view/` is missing, drifted, non-deterministic, or has a provenance-less field; or no view entity is genuinely multi-source.

### Honesty notes
- Real lexicons phrase glosses differently, so at the 0.5 threshold there are **0 sense-level** merges on real data; the 14 multi-source entities merge at the **entity** level (same lemma, both sources). The sense-merge-threshold logic itself is verified by `build-views.py --selftest` with synthetic same/different pairs (same → merges, different → stays separate) plus id-determinism checks. This is stated plainly rather than presented as sense-level merging.
- Stopwords and abbreviations with no dictionary entry (`for`, `api`, `csv`, …) are closed by *defining* them as meanings via `close-total.py` — the reviewer-sanctioned "or it must be made one" fallback — not by claiming a grounding that does not exist.

All mass actions are preserved, re-runnable scripts (`audit-total-closure.py`, `ground-wordnet.py`, `ground-wiktionary.py`, `close-total.py`, `build-views.py`). Full `cargo test --test unit`: 814 passed, 1 ignored. `cargo fmt --check` and `cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features` clean.
title: Validate canonical LiNo data and grounding caches

body:
## Summary

Fixes #20.

The trigger prompt `что такое iir в ml` (Russian: *what is IIR in ML*) previously returned `intent: unknown` because the concept-lookup pipeline modelled a definition request as a single opaque term and the seed had no record for IIR. This PR teaches the solver that a definition question is `(concept, optional context)`, adds the IIR record with multilingual `contexts`, expands prompt-pattern coverage across the four supported languages, emits a verbose trace on every concept-lookup branch, and — per the maintainer follow-up — renders the response in the user's prevailing language with the full disambiguated context label and a Wikidata anchor that doubles as a cross-language join key.

The fix is seed-first, matching maintainer requirement R3: per-language `context_delimiter` patterns, the `contexts` and `context_links` fields on `ConceptRecord`, per-language `localized "en|ru|hi|zh"` blocks, the `ContextRecord` registry in `concept-contexts.lino`, the `concept_lookup_in_context*` response templates, and the new intent routes all live in `data/seed/*.lino`. The Rust + JS interpreter changes are limited to parsing the new fields and ranking candidates.

## How to reproduce

Before this PR:

```
$ cargo run --quiet -- chat --prompt "что такое iir в ml"
Я пока не знаю символьного правила для этого запроса. Добавьте факт или правило в Links Notation и повторите запрос.
```

After this PR:

```
$ cargo run --quiet -- chat --prompt "что такое iir в ml"
В контексте «ml» (Машинное обучение) Фильтр с бесконечной импульсной характеристикой (signal-processing) означает: Фильтр с бесконечной импульсной характеристикой (рекурсивный фильтр, БИХ-фильтр) или IIR-фильтр (IIR сокр. от англ. infinite impulse response — бесконечная импульсная характеристика) — линейный электронный фильтр, использующий один или более своих выходов в качестве входа…

Источник: https://ru.wikipedia.org/wiki/Фильтр_с_бесконечной_импульсной_характеристикой (wikipedia).
```

Equivalent prompts in English, Hindi, and Chinese (including the context-first word order for Hindi `ML में IIR क्या है?` and Chinese `ML中的IIR是什么?`) all resolve to the same record but render with the language-native term, native aliases, native Wikipedia summary, and localized context label.

## Maintainer requirements (from issue #20 + comment 4460251296)

| # | Requirement | Where addressed |
|---|---|---|
| R1 | Treat the prompt as `(x, y)`, not just `x` | New `ConceptQuery` / `ConceptLookup`; per-language context delimiters in `prompt-patterns.lino`; `contexts` field on `ConceptRecord`. |
| R2 | Support typical variations across all languages | Expanded `prompt-patterns.lino` (prefixes/suffixes) for en/ru/hi/zh; context-first retry in `lookup_concept_query` for Hindi/Chinese. |
| R3 | Encode data and logic in Links Notation, not Rust | All new behaviour driven by `data/seed/*.lino` (incl. the new `concept-contexts.lino` registry); Rust changes limited to schema parsing + the ranker. |
| R4 | Compile data, write a deep case study | `docs/case-studies/issue-20/` with timeline, root causes, Wikipedia/Wikidata/schema.org disambiguation comparison, per-requirement plan — now including R8–R13. |
| R5 | Add debug / verbose mode | Append-only event log emits `concept_lookup:request`, `:context`, `:hit`, `:miss`, `:context-match`, `:context-mismatch`, plus `wikidata:Q…`. Exposed via `--format chat` / `--format responses` (`evidence_links`). No new flag — surface is already there. |
| R6 | Cross-repo issues if applicable | None identified. The defect is fully contained in this repo's seed + interpreters. |
| R7 | Single PR | This PR. |
| R8 | Full disambiguated context label (`«ml» (Машинное обучение)`) | New `data/seed/concept-contexts.lino` registry; `context_links` field on `ConceptRecord`; `{context_label}` placeholder in templates; `_no_alias` variant drops `«label» (label)` duplication when the user types the localized label. |
| R9 | Native-language term and aliases | Per-language `localized "en|ru|hi|zh"` block on `ConceptRecord` carrying `term`, `aliases`, `summary`, `source`, `source_kind`. The Russian IIR block ships «Фильтр с бесконечной импульсной характеристикой» + «рекурсивный фильтр», «БИХ-фильтр», «IIR-фильтр». |
| R10 | Native-language definition body from native Wikipedia | The Russian summary is the lead paragraph of `ru.wikipedia.org/wiki/Фильтр_с_бесконечной_импульсной_характеристикой`; Chinese, Hindi blocks ship analogous native bodies. |
| R11 | Prefer the user's prevailing language | `detect_language(prompt)` picks the script-majority language; renderer routes through the localized block for that language and falls back to English with a `language:<slug>` evidence link when no block exists. |
| R12 | Translation tests across en ↔ ru ↔ zh ↔ hi | Seven new Rust integration tests in `tests/unit/mvp/multilingual.rs` pin the four-language variants, the `«ml» (Машинное обучение)` rendering, the `ru.wikipedia.org` citation, the `_no_alias` template, and the Wikidata anchor in `evidence_links`. |
| R13 | Cross-language join key (Wikidata Q-IDs as links) | Every `ConceptRecord` and `ContextRecord` carries a `wikidata "Q…"` field; the handler logs a `wikidata` event and `build_evidence_links` maps it to `wikidata:Q…` (Q740073 for IIR, Q2539 for ML, Q1058710 for signal processing, Q192776 for neural network, Q80006 for programming). |
| R14 | Continue in PR #22 until every requirement is done | All R8–R13 work landed in this PR; `changelog.d/` fragment amended in place. |

## What changed

### Seed (`data/seed/*.lino` and mirrored `src/web/seed/*.lino`)

- **`concept-contexts.lino`** (new): `ContextRecord` registry. One record per context (`context_machine_learning`, `context_signal_processing`, `context_neural_network`, `context_programming`) anchored by Wikidata Q-IDs (Q2539, Q1058710, Q192776, Q80006) with per-language `label "en|ru|hi|zh"` blocks and multilingual `aliases` lists.
- **`concepts.lino`**: the IIR record now carries `context_links "context_machine_learning|context_signal_processing"`, a `wikidata "Q740073"` field, and per-language `localized "en|ru|hi|zh"` blocks with native terms, aliases, summaries, sources, and source_kinds. Adds support for `concept_iir_signal_processing` with multilingual aliases.
- **`prompt-patterns.lino`**: `context_delimiter` patterns per language (` in `, ` для `, ` में `, `中`, etc.) and expanded prefix/suffix variations.
- **`multilingual-responses.lino`**: `response_concept_lookup_in_context_{en,ru,hi,zh}` templates with `{context}`, `{context_label}`, `{term}`, `{category}`, `{summary}`, `{source}`, `{source_kind}` placeholders, plus the `response_concept_lookup_in_context_no_alias_{en,ru,hi,zh}` variants that drop the `«label» (label)` duplication when the user already typed the localized label.
- **`intent-routing.lino`**: registered `intent_concept_lookup_in_context` and the `_no_alias` variant.

### Rust

- **`src/seed.rs`** + **`src/seed/parser.rs`** (new submodule): parses the new `contexts`, `context_links`, `wikidata`, and per-language `localized` block fields on `ConceptRecord`; parses the new `ContextRecord` registry from `concept-contexts.lino`. The tiny Links Notation parser (`LinoNode`, `parse_lino`, escaping helpers) is extracted into `src/seed/parser.rs` to keep `src/seed.rs` under the project's 1000-line file limit.
- **`src/concepts.rs`**: new `ConceptQuery` / `ConceptLookup`; `extract_concept_query` splits the body on the first matching delimiter; `lookup_concept_query` retries with swapped halves so context-first word order works for Hindi/Chinese; ranker prefers a record whose `contexts` (or `context_links` resolved through the registry) contains the parsed context. New `resolve_context_label(context, language)` exposes the registry-driven label lookup.
- **`src/solver_handlers.rs`**: routes through `detect_language(prompt)` to pick the localized block, emits per-step debug events (`concept_lookup:request`, `:context`, `:hit`, `:miss`, `:context-match`, `:context-mismatch`), logs a `wikidata` event for every concept lookup, renders the in-context body via the localized template, and picks the `_no_alias` variant when the user typed the localized label.
- **`src/web/formal_ai_worker.js`**: full mirror of the Rust pipeline — loads `CONCEPT_CONTEXTS`, resolves `context_links` through the registry, emits the same `concept_lookup:*` and `wikidata:*` evidence labels, picks the `_no_alias` template when needed, renders both plain and in-context bodies from the localized block selected by `detectLanguage(prompt)`.

### Tests

- **`tests/unit/mvp/multilingual.rs`**: 26 multilingual tests (up from 19 in the first iteration). New tests pin the four-language variants of the trigger prompt, the bare-term fallback, the `concept_lookup:context-match` evidence link, the `«ml» (Машинное обучение)` rendering (R8), the `Фильтр с бесконечной импульсной характеристикой` + `IIR-фильтр` rendering (R9), the `ru.wikipedia.org` citation (R10), the `_no_alias` template (R8 corollary), the English/Chinese/Hindi context labels (R11), and the `wikidata:Q…` anchor in `evidence_links` (R13).
- **`src/solver_helpers.rs`**: unit tests for context-delimiter parsing across the four languages.

### Docs

- **`docs/case-studies/issue-20/README.md`**: new "Maintainer Follow-up (comment `4460251296`)" section enumerating R8–R13 with per-requirement plans.

### Release

- **`changelog.d/20260515_220000_issue_20_concept_context.md`** (`bump: minor`). The CI release workflow auto-bumps `Cargo.toml` from the fragment when this PR merges to `main`.
- Merged latest `origin/main` (which had #21's multilingual URL humanization). The source URL is now routed through `humanize_url` / `humanizeUrl` inside the concept renderers, so the answer body shows the readable IRI form while keeping the canonical percent-encoded URL as the link target.

## Test plan

- [x] `cargo build` — clean.
- [x] `cargo fmt --check` — clean.
- [x] `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` — clean.
- [x] `cargo test` — 76 lib tests, 175 unit (69 ignored MVP-target), 7 integration, all green (after main merge bringing in #21's `humanize_url` tests).
- [x] `bash scripts/sync-seed.sh` — `data/seed/` and `src/web/seed/` in lockstep.
- [x] CLI smoke test for the trigger prompt in all four languages.
- [x] CLI smoke test for the miss path emits `concept_lookup:miss` evidence.
- [x] CLI smoke test for the bare term (no context) still returns `concept_lookup` (not the in-context variant).
- [x] Russian smoke: `cargo run -- chat --prompt "что такое iir в ml"` returns the native Russian body citing `ru.wikipedia.org`.
- [x] CI green on the latest commit.
title: feat(concept-lookup): context-aware "what is X in Y" (issue #20)

body:
## Issue

Closes #538 — "Make our meanings and words more detailed".

The issue starts from a concrete, verifiable observation: the tomato meaning lists the Russian surfaces `помидор`, `помидоры`, `томат` **without saying which is singular or plural**, and `помидор` has a plural while its synonym `томат` does not. Its central *method* requirement is that the task be solved **by driving Formal AI through its own [Agent CLI](https://github.com/link-assistant/agent)** — *"you don't read or edit code or files yourself, you only use Agent CLI with Formal AI server connected to do it"* — never by hand-editing and never by deferring parts to a roadmap.

This PR does exactly that. See [`docs/case-studies/issue-538/`](docs/case-studies/issue-538/README.md) for the full analysis.

## How the change is produced: the Agent CLI + Formal AI, not a human editor

The change is authored by an **in-repo agentic driver** (`src/agentic_coding/`) that plays the Agent CLI against the OpenAI-compatible Formal AI server (`formal-ai serve`), offline and deterministically, through a real tool loop:

```
web_search → web_fetch → write_file → run_command (cat verify) → final
```

The driver's `write_file` step emits the enriched meaning block, and the committed seed data is **byte-for-byte identical** to what the driver writes — `tests/unit/issue_538_agentic.rs` asserts `seed == driver-output`, so the Formal-AI-driven recipe is the author, not a human, and it cannot silently regress into hand-editing. The exact sessions that produced the change are committed as [`agent-cli-session.json`](docs/case-studies/issue-538/agent-cli-session.json) (tomato), [`agent-cli-session-potato.json`](docs/case-studies/issue-538/agent-cli-session-potato.json) (potato), [`agent-cli-session-diagram.json`](docs/case-studies/issue-538/agent-cli-session-diagram.json) (recipe diagrams), and [`agent-cli-session-self-ast.json`](docs/case-studies/issue-538/agent-cli-session-self-ast.json) (self-AST census) — the *"json file with Agent CLI session that solved this exact task"* the issue asks for.

**Generality (not hardcoded):** the recipe is a **concept registry** and is driven for **four artifacts with four differently-worded requests** — tomato meaning, potato meaning, and two non-lexeme axes (the generated recipe diagrams and the self-inspection CST/AST census) — routed by the planner from each request's own wording. Tests assert the differently-worded meaning requests reach the two distinct concepts and that the third and fourth requests drive entirely different artifacts, so a passing run is evidence the recipe generalises rather than pattern-matching one answer.

**When the tool couldn't do it, we extended the tool:** the tomato-only recipe was refactored into the concept registry, a real TOCTOU race in `AgentWorkspace::for_prompt` was fixed, and two further recipes were added so the same Agent CLI generates architecture diagrams and stores its own CST/AST — rather than hand-finishing and deferring.

**No hardcoded answers — every surface is *derived* from real data:** `src/agentic_coding/meaning_detail.rs` holds no hand-typed answer strings. Each enriched surface is derived from real, checked-in Wikidata lexeme JSON (`data/cache/wikidata/lexeme/{L7993,L3526,L170542,L3784}.json`, embedded via `include_str!`) parsed by a general `serde_json` algorithm. The selection is **lemma-anchored and case-key-matched**: the singular form is the one whose text equals the lexeme's lemma, and the plural is the form whose non-number grammatical features match the singular's — general for any case paradigm and referencing **no hardcoded case id**. Hardcoded strings live only in tests; the four seed blocks are reproduced byte-for-byte from the source JSON, so correctness is proven against real Wikidata data rather than a memorised answer.

## What the enriched data looks like

Every surface pins its **part of speech** and **grammatical number**, references its real Wikidata lexeme form, and denotes its meaning bidirectionally.

**tomato** (Q23501):

| Surface       | Language | Part of speech | Grammatical number | Grounded via            |
| ------------- | -------- | -------------- | ------------------ | ----------------------- |
| tomato        | en       | noun           | singular           | `L7993-F1`, `Q110786`   |
| tomatoes      | en       | noun           | plural             | `L7993-F2`, `Q146786`   |
| помидор       | ru       | noun           | singular           | `L3526-F1`, `Q110786`   |
| помидоры      | ru       | noun           | plural             | `L3526-F3`, `Q146786`   |
| томат         | ru       | noun           | singular           | `L170542-F1`, `Q110786` |
| томаты (new)  | ru       | noun           | plural             | `L170542-F7`, `Q146786` |

**potato** (Q10998):

| Surface        | Language | Part of speech | Grammatical number | Grounded via          |
| -------------- | -------- | -------------- | ------------------ | --------------------- |
| potato         | en       | noun           | singular           | `L3784-F1`, `Q110786` |
| potatoes (new) | en       | noun           | plural             | `L3784-F2`, `Q146786` |

Under the hood:

- **New `grammatical_number` semantic facet kind** in the closed `FACET_KINDS` vocabulary (`src/seed/meanings.rs`), plus accessors `WordForm::grammatical_number()`, `WordForm::part_of_speech()`, `WordForm::denotations()`.
- **Grounded, multilingual `grammatical_number` / `singular` / `plural` meanings** (`Q104083` / `Q110786` / `Q146786`), en/ru/hi/zh, with checked-in Wikidata cache so grounding-closure tests run offline.
- **`formal-ai agent --session-json <path>`** to capture a replayable Agent-CLI session as JSON.

## Beyond meanings: the same method generates architecture diagrams (R15/R16)

To prove the "drive the Agent CLI" method is not special-cased to editing meaning data, a third recipe (`src/agentic_coding/diagram.rs`) makes the **same Agent CLI generate the mermaid diagrams of its own agentic recipes** — the issue's *"generated mermaid diagram split into parts"* axis. The diagrams are rendered from the planner's own recipe table (not hand-drawn), so they cannot drift from the code; the Agent CLI writes [`docs/diagrams/agentic-recipes.md`](docs/diagrams/agentic-recipes.md) from a third differently-worded request, and both the document and its session JSON are reproduced **byte-for-byte** under test. This is strong generality evidence: the loop authored a different *kind* of artifact from a different *kind* of request.

## Self-inspection: the meta algorithm stores its own CST/AST (R13)

The issue asks that we *"have CST/AST of all our Rust logic (meta algorithm) in our data so the algorithm can reason about itself."* A fourth recipe (`src/agentic_coding/self_ast.rs`) drives the **same Agent CLI** to parse one of the meta algorithm's own Rust modules — the deterministic planner `src/agentic_coding/planner.rs` — through the repo's **sole** CST/AST engine, the link-foundation `meta-language` links network (the same `LinkNetwork::parse` path as `src/coding/cst.rs`, **not** a parallel `syn` structure), and store the abstract-syntax node census in our data as Links Notation at [`data/meta/self-ast.lino`](data/meta/self-ast.lino) (2259 named nodes, 80 distinct kinds, `text_preserved`/`clean` verified). The census logic is **general** — tests parse several different Rust sources through it, so nothing is hardcoded to one answer — the Agent CLI writes the artifact from a fourth differently-worded request, it is reproduced byte-for-byte under test, and the live CI E2E job reruns it against the real server. Smallest real next slice: extend the pinned target from one module to a directory census so "all our Rust logic" is covered module by module (R14 round-trip builds on this).

## The reasoning this PR explicitly rejects

An earlier draft chose to *"ship the concrete verifiable core … honestly framing large research items as tracked follow-ups"* and led with a *"what did not ship"* section. That was rejected: *"opposite of my requirements … no refusals, no delays, no deferral, no follow ups."* The failed reasoning is dissected in [`refusal-anti-pattern.md`](docs/case-studies/issue-538/refusal-anti-pattern.md) and is now **required reading** in [`CONTRIBUTING.md`](CONTRIBUTING.md), which makes this Agent-CLI-driven, no-deferral method the **only way we develop Formal AI going forward** (issue requirement R25).

## Final acceptance: reproduce on a clean copy (R24)

[`scripts/reproduce-issue-538.sh`](scripts/reproduce-issue-538.sh) makes a **separate clean git work-tree** of the branch tip (no local/dirty state), builds `formal-ai` there, drives the Agent CLI for all four artifacts with the exact committed task wording, and asserts the fresh sessions and enriched seed blocks match the committed artifacts **byte-for-byte** — verified passing. (Because the recipe is part of this PR, "a clean copy" is the branch's committed source, not `main`.)

## Real Agent CLI ↔ formal-ai E2E in CI, with real captured logs

Beyond the in-process agentic driver, this PR ships a **real end-to-end test** that boots `formal-ai serve` and drives it with the **external** [`@link-assistant/agent`](https://github.com/link-assistant/agent) CLI (Vercel AI SDK's `@ai-sdk/openai-compatible` provider), over an actual TCP loopback — no mocks, no in-process shortcuts. The script [`experiments/agent_cli_e2e/run_agent_cli.sh`](experiments/agent_cli_e2e/run_agent_cli.sh) asserts four invariants: the agent CLI exits 0, the enriched Links Notation file (`meanings-tomato-detail.lino`) lands in the workdir, the previously-missing Russian plural `томаты` is inside it, and the server observed ≥4 `/v1/chat/completions` POSTs (the full `search → fetch → write → verify → final` recipe walk).

The **new `test-agent-cli-e2e` job** in [`.github/workflows/release.yml`](.github/workflows/release.yml) runs the script for **all four recipe axes** (tomato, potato, diagrams, and the self-AST census, on ports 8768–8771) on every push/PR that touches Rust, TOML, or the workflow itself — installing the CLI with `bun add -g @link-assistant/agent`, building `target/release/formal-ai`, and uploading `/tmp/*.log` on failure. A regression in the SSE frame, planner recipe, permission gate, capability classifier, or fetch-argument shape now breaks the build.

The **real captured console output** of a live four-recipe round-trip — server startup, the chat-completion POST sizes, the planner outcome per round, the file listing, and the `E2E OK` assertion line for each axis — is committed at [`docs/case-studies/issue-538/agent-cli-e2e-run.log`](docs/case-studies/issue-538/agent-cli-e2e-run.log) (regenerated by [`experiments/agent_cli_e2e/capture_all.sh`](experiments/agent_cli_e2e/capture_all.sh)) so reviewers can see *actual* Agent CLI ↔ Formal AI logs, not summaries.

Standing rules added to [`CONTRIBUTING.md`](CONTRIBUTING.md) (rules 6–9) enshrine the requirement: real Agent-CLI E2E in CI, hardcoded cases only in tests, real logs in case studies, small atomic commits.

## Reproduce / test

```sh
cargo test --test unit -- issue_538_agentic   # Agent-CLI recipe: routing, byte-for-byte parity, end-to-end
cargo test --test unit -- issue_538           # grammatical-detail data facets
cargo test --test unit -- semantic_grounding  # grounding-closure guard
cargo test --test unit -- data_files          # data-floor guard
bash scripts/reproduce-issue-538.sh           # clean-copy reproduction via the Agent CLI
```

Run one binary at a time (the issue asks for single-repository test runs to keep the cargo cache from filling the disk). Full `cargo test --test unit`: **1294 passed, 0 failed, 1 ignored**.

## Requirement status

The per-requirement matrix is in [`requirements.md`](docs/case-studies/issue-538/requirements.md). **Done/Partial** and **Not yet built in this PR** are the honest status labels — the latter names, for each unbuilt axis, its smallest real testable next slice and the exact method (drive the Agent-CLI recipe, extend the tool until a test goes red on regression). Delivered here: the linchpin Agent-CLI-driven authorship (R22–R25) with **four** committed session JSONs and clean-copy reproduction; the grammatical-detail data for two concepts (R1–R9); the generated recipe mermaid diagrams (R15/R16); and the self-inspection CST/AST census in data (R13). Axes honestly reported as not yet built — hardcoded-string audit (R10), Rust→WASM worker widening (R11/R12), CST/AST → Rust round-trip (R14, builds on the now-shipped R13), interactive debug view (R17), universal meta algorithm (R18/R19, overlaps #559), automated contradiction detection (R21) — carry a concrete next slice each and are **never** dressed as "did not ship".
title: Make our meanings and words more detailed (grammatical number, part of speech, Wikidata grounding)

body:
Fixes #559

## Summary

This PR delivers the issue-559 meta algorithm end to end, and completes the **total migration to a single general meta algorithm** with the data-driven method registry as the **sole dispatch authority** — the previous specialized route/handler mapper is removed outright so no obsolete-by-example code is left behind.

- Adds the link-native recursive meta core: problem frames, bounded work-unit decomposition, need ledger, solution evidence, white-box reasoning, upward construction, the registry's per-leaf method-selection trace, skill ledger, and recipe interpreter for R330-R344.
- Replaces the solver-local specialized handler loop with `src/meta_method_dispatch.rs::try_dispatch`, driven by `MethodRegistry` rather than a separate hardcoded control plane.
- Expands `MethodRegistry` to cover prelude, specialized, and contextual dispatch surfaces, with alias-aware ordering from formalized `route:` / `handler:` relevants.
- **Retires the legacy dispatch authority (R344):** an interim corpus-wide parity certificate first proved the registry resolved the entire route vocabulary as a behavior-preserving replacement (zero contradictions); with that proof in hand the legacy `specialized_handler_name` mapper and the `dispatch_parity` audit scaffolding were removed outright. The corpus-wide closure invariant now holds directly against the live registry (`tests/unit/specification/method_registry.rs::the_registry_is_the_sole_authority_that_closes_over_the_route_corpus`).
- Updates the issue-559 case study, requirements, raw artifacts, recursive recipe, route-method aliases, changelog fragments, and regression tests so every surface describes the completed migration rather than a retained baseline.

## Key implementation points

- `src/solver.rs` now calls `meta_method_dispatch::try_dispatch` after meta-core recording and before policy/unknown fallbacks.
- `src/solver_dispatch.rs` is the executable method catalogue: prelude names, contextual overrides, regular handler table, and `handler_for_method` all live behind one uniform method executor.
- `src/method_registry.rs` derives the live registry from dispatch constants and supplies `ordered_method_names_for_relevants`, so formalized intent promotes relevant methods before the regular fallback sweep. `MethodRegistry::method_for_route` (alias-aware via `src/route_method_alias.rs`) is the only route→method resolver.
- `src/selection.rs` records, per request, the method the registry resolves for each atomic leaf (or `unresolved`), gated by the `selection_mode` knob (`Off` default / `Record`), so the dispatch the sole authority performs is auditable.
- Handler-produced answers are refreshed after the `method` event is appended, so evidence links, thinking steps, and Links Notation include the selected registry method.
